title: Academy Co-ordinator
description_text:
Job Title: Academy Co-ordinator 
Salary: £22,222 – £24,000 per annum 
Hours per week: 35 hours
Location: At IOSH, we embrace hybrid working because we believe it's the key to achieving work-life balance, strategic success and fostering our collaborative culture. You’ll be required to work from our head office in Wigston, Leicester a minimum of 2 days per week. The remainder of your working hours will be undertaken from home.
Closing date: 27 February 2026
About the role
This is a pivotal role at the heart of the IOSH Academy, where no two days are the same. You’ll play a key part in keeping things running smoothly while helping us hit our enrolment targets. How? By connecting with prospective learners through phone calls, emails, and both face-to-face and virtual meetings—and turning curiosity into commitment.
Customer experience is everything here. You’ll be the friendly first voice learners hear, guiding them from that very first enquiry all the way through to enrolment and IOSH membership. And it doesn’t stop there—you’ll continue to support learners throughout their journey, making sure every interaction feels seamless, supportive, and positive.
What you’ll bring
You’re confident, proactive, and love being customer-facing. As the first point of contact for potential learners, you’ll enjoy answering questions, building rapport, and turning enquiries into enrolments. With a background in customer service, you’ll be comfortable picking up the phone, making outgoing calls, and confidently guiding people towards the right learning opportunity.
You’ll thrive working towards targets and feel comfortable managing your own workload, using your initiative to get results. Strong IT skills are a must—you’ll be sending offers, responding to email enquiries, and setting learners up on the LMS with ease. If you enjoy juggling tasks, meeting goals, and making a real difference to people’s learning journeys, you’ll feel right at home.
Essential criteria
· Confident in making outgoing calls to customers. 
· Significant experience in sales focused customer service and telephone roles
· Understanding of customer service systems and sales processes 
· Confident in using Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint. 
How to apply
To apply, you'll need an up to date CV and some additional time to complete the application questions so we can find out more about you and your experience. Just click on the apply button below.

If you have any queries about the role or recruitment process please reach out to the recruiting manager or the People team.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Academy Co-ordinator will connect with prospective learners through various communication channels and help them through the enrolment process. They will also provide ongoing support to learners throughout their journey.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have significant experience in sales-focused customer service and be confident in making outgoing calls. Strong IT skills and the ability to manage workloads and meet targets are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: We're the Chartered body and leading membership organisation for safety and health professionals.
linkedin_org_locations: The Grange, Highfield Drive, Wigston, Leicestershire LE18 1NN, GB
linkedin_org_description:
The Institution of Occupational Safety and Health (IOSH) is the world’s leading professional body for people responsible for safety and health in the workplace. We act as a champion, adviser, advocate and trainer for safety and health professionals working in organisations of all sizes.

We set the standard for the profession, supporting members with resources, events, news and guidance. Chartered Membership of IOSH is recognised worldwide as the hallmark of professional excellence in workplace safety and health.

IOSH Training and Skills courses, such as Working Safely, Managing Safely and Leading Safety, are delivered by a network of approved training providers to over 179,000 delegates each year. 

As an employer, IOSH offers a supportive workplace which values and encourages flexibility, integrity, excellence and a One IOSH approach to everything we do. 

Get in touch with us here: https://iosh.com/contact

Find and follow us on other social media channels:
● Facebook: IOSHOfficial
● Instagram: IOSHOfficial
● TikTok: @IOSHOfficial
● X: @IOSH_tweets

title: Junior Consultant Emergency Medicine-Government Hospital in UAE
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is a respected human resources consulting firm dedicated to connecting skilled healthcare professionals with leading medical institutions. We are currently seeking a Junior Consultant in Emergency Medicine for a prestigious government hospital in the UAE. In this role, you will provide essential emergency medical services and support to patients in a fast-paced clinical environment.
As a Junior Consultant in Emergency Medicine, you will work closely with experienced medical professionals to assess, diagnose, and treat patients with acute and potentially life-threatening conditions. This position will allow you to enhance your clinical skills and gain valuable experience while contributing to the health and well-being of the community.
Completion of an internship or residency in Emergency Medicine
Basic experience in managing patients in emergency settings, preferably in a hospital environment
Strong clinical assessment and decision-making skills
Ability to perform essential emergency procedures
Excellent communication skills and a compassionate approach to patient care
Willingness to learn and develop skills under the guidance of senior physicians
Team-oriented mindset with the ability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary environment
Commitment to ongoing education and professional improvement
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Junior Consultant in Emergency Medicine, you will provide essential emergency medical services and support to patients in a fast-paced clinical environment. You will work closely with experienced medical professionals to assess, diagnose, and treat patients with acute and potentially life-threatening conditions.
ai_requirements_summary: Completion of an internship or residency in Emergency Medicine is required, along with basic experience in managing patients in emergency settings. Strong clinical assessment and decision-making skills, as well as excellent communication skills, are essential.
linkedin_org_locations: Office 113, Al Khaimah Building, Port Saeed Deira, Dubai UAE Tel:  00971 4 2555465, Deira, Dubai 25570, AE

title: Python Tools Programmer - 6 Month FTC
description_text:
Supermassive Games have produced some of the most recognisable and compelling storytelling titles in the world of AAA games. The BAFTA-winning Until Dawn, the cinematic masterclass of The Dark Pictures Anthology, the multi-award winning The Quarry, the action-packed rollercoaster horror VR titles Switchback VR and Rush of Blood to the world of Little Nightmares, and many more! 
Time and time again, our incredibly talented and passionate team have delivered games that strike fear into all that dare to play. 
We’re now on the hunt for a Python Tools Programmer to join us here at Supermassive Games. Fancy joining our team and creating your own story? 
This is a 6 Month fixed term contract to start March 6th 2026 (We wont be able to offer sponsorship for this vacancy)
In this role you’ll be:
As a Python Tools Programmer, you will support multiple game projects by developing and maintaining content creation tools and workflows. You’ll collaborate directly with game teams to understand their needs, develop extensible systems and provide ongoing support.
You will be involved in maintaining and improving existing Python Tools, adding features to support new requirements, and architecting solutions to new problems.
A typical day will look like:
Working with a small team to develop Python tools for content creators on game teams.
Documenting tool usage and supporting game teams in adoption of new systems.
Meeting regularly with stakeholders to report on progress and receive feedback.
Creating technical designs for solutions and collaborating with technical peers to address feedback.
Planning large deliverables, breaking them down into smaller tasks and providing time estimates.
Responding to bug reports or support requests, working closely with colleagues on game teams to resolve issues.
Background and skills that would best suit this position:
Experience developing and owning complex content creation tools and pipelines in Python, from end to end - requirements gathering, design and planning, implementation, support and iteration.
Experience debugging Python tools in DCC Applications.
Experience with managing dependencies and testing compatibility between product versions.
Experience with version control systems such as Perforce or Git
Professional attitude towards support, including face-to-face support, progress updates and high-quality documentation
Experience working with Unreal Engine, Blueprint scripting, Unreal's Python API and/or C++ coding would be beneficial
Experience automating content creator workflows in DCC tools (Maya, MotionBuilder etc.) would be beneficial.
The Perks of being a Supermassive Superstar are:
25 Days Annual Leave (3 of which are used when we close the studio for our end of year break) 
Private Medical Insurance (BUPA) 
Medicash assistance (Including dental and optician support)
Life Assurance (6 x annual salary) 
Pension Scheme 
Employee Assistance Programme 
Income Protection 
Enhanced Family Leave (maternity and paternity pay)
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Python Tools Programmer, you will support multiple game projects by developing and maintaining content creation tools and workflows. You will collaborate directly with game teams to understand their needs and provide ongoing support.
ai_requirements_summary: Experience in developing and owning complex content creation tools and pipelines in Python is essential. Familiarity with Unreal Engine and DCC tools is beneficial.

65 Woodbridge Road, Guildford, Surrey GU1 4RD, GB
linkedin_org_description: Founded in 2008, we are a BAFTA-winning game studio, developing innovative storytelling games. We have released a number of successful titles and are best known for the critically acclaimed PS4 hit Until Dawn, The Dark Pictures Anthology - a series of stand-alone, horror games - and teen-horror game The Quarry. We are currently working on Directive 8020 and Little Nightmares III. Our latest release is The Casting of Frank Stone - a haunting horror game set in the Dead by Daylight universe.

title: Growth Manager | Digital & Performance Marketing | Kuala Lumpur-based
organization: Vita Green Health Product Company Limited
locations_alt_raw: Brickfields, Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description_text:
About Vita Green Group
Vita Green stands at the forefront of the health supplements industry, committed to enhancing well-being through innovative research and cutting-edge solutions. Our GMP and HACCP-certified facilities ensure top-tier quality control at every step—from sourcing premium raw materials to advanced R&D—guaranteeing excellence in every product. With a diverse portfolio of established brands and over 150 high-quality products, we are continually expanding our reach into markets worldwide. At Vita Green, we uphold the highest standards, paving the way for a healthier future for our customers and communities.
We are seeking a motivated Growth Manager to lead our Direct-to-Consumer (D2C) market expansion efforts in Malaysia. This role will focus on developing and executing our e-commerce strategy through platforms like Lazada, managing paid media, and driving performance marketing initiatives to achieve our revenue growth goals.
What You'll Be Doing
Develop and implement comprehensive D2C growth strategies for the Malaysian market on Lazada.
Manage and optimize paid media campaigns across various platforms to maximize performance and ROI.
Analyze key performance metrics and adjust strategies accordingly to meet revenue targets.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance product offerings and customer experiences.
Monitor market trends, consumer behavior, and competitor activities to inform strategic decisions.
Create and manage budgets for growth initiatives, ensuring effective allocation of resources.
Communicate strategies and reports to stakeholders, providing insights and recommendations for improvement.
Who we’re looking for
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
Extensive experience in e-commerce growth or digital marketing, preferably in the D2C space.
Strong background in performance marketing, including PPC, social media advertising, and SEO.
Proven track record of driving revenue growth in a competitive market.
Excellent analytical skills with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in Chinese and English.
Familiarity with Lazada or other e-commerce platforms in Southeast Asia is a plus.
Why Join Us
We value your professional and personal growth – fostering a positive work culture is our priority. Here, you’ll find more than just a job, you’ll discover a genuine opportunity to thrive. 
Be part of a forward-thinking company dedicated to health and wellness. 
Engage in pioneering projects that push the boundaries of innovation.
Opportunities for professional growth and development.
Generous leave policies, including paid annual leave, birthday leave, and other special leaves
Collaborate with a talented and diverse team.
Attractive staff purchase discount from your first day at work.
And more!!!!
We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. 
Join us in our mission to make a positive impact on wellness around the world!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Growth Manager will develop and implement D2C growth strategies for the Malaysian market, focusing on e-commerce platforms like Lazada. They will manage paid media campaigns and analyze performance metrics to drive revenue growth.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in Marketing or a related field and extensive experience in e-commerce growth or digital marketing. A strong background in performance marketing and excellent analytical and communication skills in Chinese and English are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: 18 Dai Hei St, Vita Green Tower, Tai Po District, HK
30 Canton Road, Silvercord Tower 1, Hong Kong, HK
linkedin_org_description:
From our humble beginnings in 1993, Vita Green has transformed into a significant force in the global healthcare sector. We're rapidly nearing the milestone of 1,000 employees, indicative of our persistent growth and expansive reach. Our dynamic team operates across three GMP-certified pharmaceutical factories and six international offices, underlining our comprehensive global footprint.

We've firmly established our market presence with over 70 specialty shops spread across Asia. In addition, our broad and inventive product portfolio, encompassing 13 proprietary brands and more than 150 distinctive products, has not only won widespread acclaim throughout Asia, but has also captivated interest in the wider markets of Europe and the USA.

Vita Green is committed to rigorously maintaining PICS/GMP standards, showcasing our unwavering commitment to the quality, safety, and efficacy of every product we offer. This steadfast dedication, in tandem with our consistent expansion, solidifies our status as a formidable player in the international healthcare industry. Our ongoing mission remains the same - to enhance global public health, one high-quality product at a time.

title: AI Enablement Specialist
description_text:
The AI Enablement Specialist is responsible for ensuring that the organization doesn’t just deploy AI technology, but 
successfully integrates it into daily operations. While the delivery teams build the tools, this role ensures the workforce is ready, 
willing, and able to use them. You will act as the primary bridge between technical AI development and the end-users, focusing 
on human-centric adoption, prompt engineering excellence, and measurable productivity gains.
The AI Enablement Specialist promotes the Group's AI vision and strategic objectives through clear company wide 
communication and adoption initiatives with measurable value. In collaboration with AI development teams and regional
stakeholders, they drive the integration and adoption of new AI technologies across the organisation, ensuring a coordinated 
approach to AI solution delivery that maximises enterprise value and adoption.
The primary goals of the Group AI team include:
● Leading the definition and communication of the AI Products and technologies.
● Developing and maintaining a roadmap of products and initiatives.
● Gathering and analyzing needs and business requirements to identify technology solutions.
● Provide training and governance around the usage and development of AI solutions across the business.
● Ensuring AI product quality and alignment with user and business needs.
● Monitoring and analyzing product performance and user feedback to drive iterative improvements.
● Remain up to date with modern AI and Data trends and business opportunities.
To ensure the successful adoption and long-term value realization of AI tools &
initiatives by:
● Acting as the "AI Translator": Converting technical AI capabilities into practical, department-specific use cases for non-
technical staff.
● Driving AI Fluency: Designing and delivering training programs that move employees from basic awareness to advanced AI 
proficiency.
● Optimizing Workflows: Partnering with regional leads to identify "bottleneck" processes that can be streamlined through AI-
augmented workflows.
● Ensuring Ethical Use: Promoting and governing the safe, compliant, and ethical use of AI tools across the organization.
● AI Communication: Sharing opportunities and advancements in AI within the organisation through a trusted network of AI evangelists
Adoption Strategy & Planning: Define and execute an "Enablement Roadmap," ensuring AI tools are rolled out with clear change 
management plans and support structures.
● AI Pioneers: Develop and collaborate with a large network of AI evangelists across the organisation to create a culture of 
learning and knowledge sharing, from which to further reach business value and AI Adoption.
● Prompt Engineering & Best Practices: Develop and maintain a central library of "gold standard" prompts and templates for 
various business functions (e.g., Sales, HR, Finance).
● Stakeholder Engagement: Collaborate with regional leads to gather user feedback, identify "AI Champions" within 
departments, and address resistance to AI adoption.
● Value Realization & ROI Tracking: Define and monitor adoption KPIs (e.g., active user rates, time-saved metrics) to articulate 
the cumulative business value generated by AI investments.
● Training & Education: Lead workshops, webinars, and 1-on-1 coaching sessions to improve the organization's collective "AI IQ."
● Governance & Compliance: Champion AI governance frameworks, ensuring users adhere to GDPR, the EU AI Act, and 
internal data privacy policies when interacting with AI tools.
● Iterative Improvement: Monitor user feedback to provide the central AI team with actionable insights for product refinement 
and feature requests.
Experience
● Proven experience in Change Management, Instructional Design, or Digital Transformation roles.
● Experience in a corporate setting driving the adoption of new technologies (highly desirable).
● Demonstrated experience in a client-facing or internal consulting role.
● Experience with AI/ML tools (e.g., Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot) in a professional context.
● Experience working with partners on suppliers on planning and program delivery.
● AI Literacy: Strong understanding of Generative AI concepts, LLM capabilities, and prompt engineering techniques.
● Communication: Exceptional ability to translate complex technical concepts into simple, persuasive language for diverse 
audiences.
● Analytical thinking: Ability to analyze business requirements and advise on scope for operational improvement through AI.
● Influence: Ability to build effective relationships and influence stakeholders who may be skeptical of AI technology.
Knowledge & Skills
● Autonomy: Works under broad direction. Is fully responsible for meeting allocated objectives. Establishes milestones and has 
a significant role in the assignment of tasks and/or responsibilities.
● Influence: Influences organisation, customers, suppliers, partners, and peers on the contribution of their own specialism. 
Builds appropriate and effective business relationships. Makes decisions that impact the success of assigned work, i.e. results, 
deadlines, and budget. Has significant influence over the allocation and management of resources appropriate to given 
assignments.
● Complexity: Performs an extensive range and variety of complex professional work activities. Undertakes work that requires 
the application of fundamental principles in a wide and often unpredictable range of contexts. Understands the relationship 
between own specialism and wider customer/organisational requirements.
● Business Skills: Advises on the available standards, methods, tools and applications relevant to own specialism and can make 
appropriate choices from alternatives. Analyses, designs, plans, executes and evaluates work to time, cost and quality targets. 
Assesses and evaluates risk. Communicates effectively, both formally and informally. Demonstrates leadership. Facilitates 
collaboration between stakeholders who have diverse objectives. Take all requirements into account when making proposals. 
Take initiative to keep skills up to date. Mentors colleagues. Maintains an awareness of developments in the industry.
● Analytical: Analyses requirements and advises on scope and options for continuous operational improvement. Demonstrates 
creativity, innovation, and ethical thinking in applying solutions for the benefit of the customer/stakeholder.
Desirable
● Scrum / Agile qualifications
● Prosci or similar Change Management certification.
● Certifications in AI/Data literacy or Prompt Engineering.
ai_core_responsibilities: The AI Enablement Specialist ensures successful integration of AI technology into daily operations and acts as a bridge between technical development and end-users. They drive the adoption of AI technologies, focusing on human-centric adoption and measurable productivity gains.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have proven experience in Change Management, Instructional Design, or Digital Transformation roles, preferably in a corporate setting. Strong understanding of AI concepts and exceptional communication skills are essential for translating technical concepts for diverse audiences.

Rentokil Initial plc employs c.68,500 people across 89 countries - offering the experience and expertise of a multi-national organisation, whilst delivering services with the agility and characteristics of a local business.

title: Trainee Solicitor - Manchester/Leeds
description_text:
About us 
Stone King has supported clients and communities since 1785, and while our heritage spans more than two centuries, we continue to embrace change and invest in the future.
Much of our work directly supports charities, schools, social enterprises, faith organisations, public bodies, businesses, and individuals who are building stronger communities and driving positive societal change.
Our collaborative culture and core values are intertwined with our ethos of doing the right thing by our clients, communities, and our people. Our passion for the sectors we work in and the purpose-driven work we do with our clients has resulted in SK being recognised as national leaders in our specialist fields across the country.
We embrace individuality and difference, because diverse perspectives make us stronger, more creative and more human. Our people matter, and that means giving every colleague the chance to make a real contribution to who we are, what we do, and how we shape the future of the firm.
At Stone King, our qualified solicitors are more than just lawyers: they are client experts, problem‑solvers, communicators, collaborators, and trusted partners to clients navigating pivotal moments in their lives and businesses.
The opportunity 
We have an opportunity for a Trainee Solicitor to join us here at Stone King in our Manchester and Leeds offices for commencement in September 2026, March 2027 and September 2027. This role will be split between both offices.
The legal profession is evolving and redefining what it means to be a lawyer. At Stone King, we are embracing this change and invest in developing future‑ready lawyers who can adapt, lead and make a meaningful impact. 
Being a solicitor isn’t easy. It means navigating intellectually and emotionally demanding situations, supporting clients through some of the most challenging moments of their lives, and showing up with professionalism and compassion in both day‑to‑day work and in‑person events. But the opportunity to make a meaningful difference to people’s lives is profoundly rewarding, and it’s at the heart of why our work matters.
If you’re looking for a training contract that prepares you to thrive in a changing world and gives you the chance to make a meaningful difference along the way, you’ll find it here. 
Your Training 
While you’ll gain exposure across the firm’s sectors, our Manchester/Leeds training contract will typically sit within the Charities and Social Enterprise pathway. The training contract may include seats in Ecclesiastical Property, Employment, Charity and Social Enterprise, and Dispute Resolution
This structure gives you meaningful depth within a core area while still ensuring broad experience. It helps you understand client needs from multiple perspectives, develop strong client‑care skills, and gain the well‑rounded foundation you need to become a trusted adviser.
An enhanced 30 month training contract, giving you the time giving you time to grow confidently, thoughtfully and without compromise.
Development in key skills: We invest in training that gives you the confidence to build relationships, communicate your value and understand the wider commercial landscape our clients operate in, skills increasingly essential for tomorrow’s lawyers.
AI‑enhanced learning and assessment. We embrace technology, including in our recruitment. Instead of policing AI use, we’ll test your judgement, adaptability and critical thinking. Throughout your contract, you’ll have opportunities to build emerging capabilities in AI and legal tech through hands‑on work in our AI Hub.
Reverse mentoring programme with the Board, giving you the chance to shape our firm’s strategic direction while strengthening your own confidence, communication and leadership skills. 
Presenting to our Partnership Council, a rare opportunity to contribute to firmwide decision‑making. Your voice matters here.
Opportunities to build emerging capabilities in AI and legal technology, preparing you for a rapidly evolving profession. 
Paid volunteering and community focused work is embedded into your training. Social purpose sits at the heart of Stone King. You’ll take part in community projects and pro bono initiatives that connect directly with our firm’s values and commitment to social impact. 
A genuinely supportive, people-first culture, where development and wellbeing are at the forefront. You’ll be surrounded by people who want you to thrive. 
We accept applications from both those on the LPC and SQE pathways. See more detail around our qualification offering on our website. Whether you’re starting your legal journey for the first time or bringing experience from another career, we welcome the unique perspectives you can offer.
Essential 
Minimum 2:2 degree (or equivalent) in law, or a non‑law degree supported by a recognised legal conversion course.
A genuine interest in Stone King—our work, our clients, and the sectors we serve.
Alignment to our core values, TRUST. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Demonstrated emotional resilience and composure, able to support clients through challenging or sensitive circumstances. 
A collaborative and adaptable mindset, able to work effectively with colleagues and clients in a fast‑paced environment.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills.
Good organisational skills, with the ability to manage competing deadlines and shifting priorities.
A willingness to learn and develop.
Desirable 
Experience that demonstrates alignment with one or more of our practice areas.
Can demonstrate a commitment to making a meaningful impact, motivated by the opportunity to positively influence clients’ lives and contribute to the wider purpose of the organisation.
Prior exposure to legal environments (e.g., vacation schemes, paralegal work, legal clinics, mini‑pupillages, or shadowing).
Demonstrated interest in charity, education, faith‑based, or social enterprise sectors—through volunteering, research, extracurriculars, or employment.
Evidence of strong client‑care skills, particularly in dealing with vulnerable individuals or sensitive issues.
The recruitment process 
Details of the recruitment process can be found on our website here. 
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
Individual differences are embraced at Stone King, and we strive to create an environment in which people feel supported and able to be themselves. We are committed to promoting an inclusive working environment that supports the growth and development for all. We are proud to be signatories of the Mindful Business Charter, the Race Fairness Commitment, and the Age Friendly Employer Pledge, and we are a Disability Confident Employer.
Stone King LLP is committed to offering an interview to any job applicant that declares they have a disability and meets the minimum criteria for the role as defined by the job description and person specification. A full copy of the person specification and job description can be provided by contacting our Recruitment team. In some recruitment situations such as high-peak times, we might need to limit the overall numbers of interviews we offer. This will include the number of interviews offered to disabled people who meet the minimum criteria. When that happens, we’ll still aim to offer a proportionate number of interviews to disabled applicants.
We aim to make our recruitment process as inclusive as possible and will make reasonable adjustments where needed. To discuss adjustments or request information in an alternative format, please contact our recruitment team at Recruitment@stoneking.co.uk or 01225 326770.
Stone King LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to ensuring equality of opportunity by encouraging all our staff to achieve their full potential and guaranteeing that all employment decisions are taken without reference to irrelevant information or discriminatory practices.
Profit Share Scheme
Cycle to work scheme
Private Medical Insurance
Life assurance (4 x salary)
Pension scheme
28 days holiday (in addition to statutory UK holidays)
Additional day allocated for CSR activities per annum
Two weeks' celebration leave after qualification 
Additional study leave and support for SQE 
Closing date: 9 April 2026
The advert will close at 5.30pm on this date.
Stone King LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to ensuring equality of opportunity by encouraging all our staff to achieve their full potential and guaranteeing that all employment decisions are taken without reference to irrelevant information or discriminatory practices.

Two Weeks' Celebration Leave After Qualification
Additional Study Leave And Support For SQE

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a minimum 2:2 degree in law or a non-law degree with a legal conversion course. A genuine interest in Stone King and alignment with their core values are essential.

linkedin_org_description:
We have built a name for excellence with our commercial, private and public sector clients. Our industry focus, including our leading reputation in the charity and education sectors, enables us to provide tailored advice specific to your needs.

Because technical ability alone is not enough, we will seek to build exceptional working relationships with you. By doing so, we will develop a deep understanding of your issues to create better commercial outcomes.

We offer specialist legal advice delivered in a positive and personable manner.

title: Trainee Solicitor - Cambridge
description_text:
About us 
Stone King has supported clients and communities since 1785, and while our heritage spans more than two centuries, we continue to embrace change and invest in the future.
Much of our work directly supports charities, schools, social enterprises, faith organisations, public bodies, businesses, and individuals who are building stronger communities and driving positive societal change.
Our collaborative culture and core values are intertwined with our ethos of doing the right thing by our clients, communities, and our people. Our passion for the sectors we work in and the purpose-driven work we do with our clients has resulted in SK being recognised as national leaders in our specialist fields across the country.
We embrace individuality and difference, because diverse perspectives make us stronger, more creative and more human. Our people matter, and that means giving every colleague the chance to make a real contribution to who we are, what we do, and how we shape the future of the firm.
At Stone King, our qualified solicitors are more than just lawyers: they are client experts, problem‑solvers, communicators, collaborators, and trusted partners to clients navigating pivotal moments in their lives and businesses.
The opportunity 
We have an opportunity for a Trainee Solicitor to join us here at Stone King in our Cambridge office for commencement in September 2026, March 2027 and September 2027. 
The legal profession is evolving and redefining what it means to be a lawyer. At Stone King, we are embracing this change and invest in developing future‑ready lawyers who can adapt, lead and make a meaningful impact. 
Being a solicitor isn’t easy. It means navigating intellectually and emotionally demanding situations, supporting clients through some of the most challenging moments of their lives, and showing up with professionalism and compassion in both day‑to‑day work and in‑person events. But the opportunity to make a meaningful difference to people’s lives is profoundly rewarding, and it’s at the heart of why our work matters.
If you’re looking for a training contract that prepares you to thrive in a changing world and gives you the chance to make a meaningful difference along the way, you’ll find it here. 
Your Training 
While you’ll gain exposure across the firm’s sectors, our Cambridge training contract will typically sit within the Public Sector pathway. The training contract may include seats in Employment, Immigration, Education, and Corporate & Commercial. 
This structure gives you meaningful depth within a core area while still ensuring broad experience. It helps you understand client needs from multiple perspectives, develop strong client‑care skills, and gain the well‑rounded foundation you need to become a trusted adviser.
An enhanced 30 month training contract, giving you the time giving you time to grow confidently, thoughtfully and without compromise.
Development in key skills: We invest in training that gives you the confidence to build relationships, communicate your value and understand the wider commercial landscape our clients operate in, skills increasingly essential for tomorrow’s lawyers.
AI‑enhanced learning and assessment. We embrace technology, including in our recruitment. Instead of policing AI use, we’ll test your judgement, adaptability and critical thinking. Throughout your contract, you’ll have opportunities to build emerging capabilities in AI and legal tech through hands‑on work in our AI Hub.
Reverse mentoring programme with the Board, giving you the chance to shape our firm’s strategic direction while strengthening your own confidence, communication and leadership skills. 
Presenting to our Partnership Council, a rare opportunity to contribute to firmwide decision‑making. Your voice matters here.
Opportunities to build emerging capabilities in AI and legal technology, preparing you for a rapidly evolving profession. 
Paid volunteering and community focused work is embedded into your training. Social purpose sits at the heart of Stone King. You’ll take part in community projects and pro bono initiatives that connect directly with our firm’s values and commitment to social impact. 
A genuinely supportive, people-first culture, where development and wellbeing are at the forefront. You’ll be surrounded by people who want you to thrive. 
We accept applications from both those on the LPC and SQE pathways. See more detail around our qualification offering on our website. Whether you’re starting your legal journey for the first time or bringing experience from another career, we welcome the unique perspectives you can offer.
Essential 
Minimum 2:2 degree (or equivalent) in law, or a non‑law degree supported by a recognised legal conversion course.
A genuine interest in Stone King—our work, our clients, and the sectors we serve.
Alignment to our core values, TRUST. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Demonstrated emotional resilience and composure, able to support clients through challenging or sensitive circumstances. 
A collaborative and adaptable mindset, able to work effectively with colleagues and clients in a fast‑paced environment.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills.
Good organisational skills, with the ability to manage competing deadlines and shifting priorities.
A willingness to learn and develop.
Desirable 
Experience that demonstrates alignment with one or more of our practice areas.
Can demonstrate a commitment to making a meaningful impact, motivated by the opportunity to positively influence clients’ lives and contribute to the wider purpose of the organisation.
Prior exposure to legal environments (e.g., vacation schemes, paralegal work, legal clinics, mini‑pupillages, or shadowing).
Demonstrated interest in charity, education, faith‑based, or social enterprise sectors—through volunteering, research, extracurriculars, or employment.
Evidence of strong client‑care skills, particularly in dealing with vulnerable individuals or sensitive issues.
The recruitment process 
Details of the recruitment process can be found on our website here. 
Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
Individual differences are embraced at Stone King, and we strive to create an environment in which people feel supported and able to be themselves. We are committed to promoting an inclusive working environment that supports the growth and development for all. We are proud to be signatories of the Mindful Business Charter, the Race Fairness Commitment, and the Age Friendly Employer Pledge, and we are a Disability Confident Employer.
Stone King LLP is committed to offering an interview to any job applicant that declares they have a disability and meets the minimum criteria for the role as defined by the job description and person specification. A full copy of the person specification and job description can be provided by contacting our Recruitment team. In some recruitment situations such as high-peak times, we might need to limit the overall numbers of interviews we offer. This will include the number of interviews offered to disabled people who meet the minimum criteria. When that happens, we’ll still aim to offer a proportionate number of interviews to disabled applicants.
We aim to make our recruitment process as inclusive as possible and will make reasonable adjustments where needed. To discuss adjustments or request information in an alternative format, please contact our recruitment team at Recruitment@stoneking.co.uk or 01225 326770.

title: Junior Marketing Assistant
description_text:
SASMAR is seeking an enthusiastic and proactive Junior Marketing Assistant to join our marketing team. As a prominent pharmaceutical company specializing in personal care and fertility products, including our well-known Conceive Plus brand, we are dedicated to driving innovation and growth in our market.
In this entry-level role, you will provide vital support to our marketing efforts by assisting with various projects and tasks that enhance brand visibility and engagement. This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in marketing and contribute to our mission of offering quality products that benefit our customers.
Key Responsibilities
Assist in the implementation of marketing campaigns and activities across various channels.
Help create and manage marketing materials, including brochures, presentations, and promotional content.
Conduct market research to gather insights on industry trends, competitors, and customer preferences.
Support social media management by scheduling posts, engaging with audiences, and monitoring analytics.
Coordinate logistics for marketing events, trade shows, and product launches.
Assist with administrative tasks related to the marketing department, including maintaining databases and tracking budgets.
Collaborate with team members on brainstorming sessions and creative initiatives to promote our brand.
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is preferred.
Some experience or internships in marketing or related areas is a plus, but not required.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Basic understanding of digital marketing and social media platforms.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
A positive attitude and willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment.
Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team.
Fluency in English; additional languages are a bonus.
Competitive salary
English language and multi-cultural work environment
Private Health Insurance (medical, dental, hospitalisation)
Career advancement opportunities in stable company
Modern offices, convenient to public transport.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Junior Marketing Assistant will assist in implementing marketing campaigns and managing marketing materials. They will also conduct market research and support social media management.
ai_requirements_summary: A bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is preferred, along with strong communication skills. Basic understanding of digital marketing and proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite are also required.

Our dedicated and growing teams are committed to improving the health and well being of consumers by providing products of superior quality and value. Incorporating the latest advancements in over the counter and self medication in the areas of fertility, family planning, women's health and personal care.

title: Flutter Development Intern
description_text:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Location: Gurgaon
JobType: full-time
We are seeking a motivated Flutter Development Intern with foundational knowledge of Flutter or hands-on experience through academic, personal, or internship projects. The intern will collaborate closely with engineering and product teams to build, test, and enhance cross-platform mobile applications. This role offers strong exposure to real-world development practices and production-level features.
Key Responsibilities
Assist in developing cross-platform mobile applications using Flutter (Dart) 
Implement UI designs to ensure a smooth and responsive user experience 
Integrate REST APIs and manage basic backend interactions 
Debug, test, and optimize application performance 
Collaborate with designers and developers to deliver product features 
Participate in code reviews and follow established coding standards 
Write clean, reusable, and well-documented code 
Required Skills & Qualifications
Basic knowledge of Flutter and Dart 
Experience working on at least one Flutter project (academic, personal, or internship) 
Understanding of Flutter widgets, layouts, and state management fundamentals 
Familiarity with REST APIs and JSON 
Ability to understand and work with an existing codebase 
Exposure to Firebase or third-party SDK integrations 
Basic knowledge of Git or other version control systems 
GitHub portfolio or sample Flutter applications preferred 
Strong problem-solving skills with a willingness to learn 
Eligibility
Final-year students 
Fresh graduates 
Self-taught developers with practical Flutter project experience 
What You Will Gain
Hands-on experience in Flutter mobile app development 
Mentorship from experienced developers 
Exposure to production-grade mobile applications 
Internship completion certificate
ai_core_responsibilities: The intern will assist in developing cross-platform mobile applications using Flutter and implement UI designs for a smooth user experience. They will also collaborate with designers and developers to deliver product features and participate in code reviews.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have basic knowledge of Flutter and Dart, with experience on at least one Flutter project. Familiarity with REST APIs, JSON, and basic knowledge of Git is also required.

title: Head of Business Intelligence
description_text:
Future is a global, data-first platform powered by technology and enabled by our trusted specialist brands. We are currently evolving our Business Intelligence (BI) capability through a strategic partnering approach, designed to deeply integrate data expertise within our B2C, B2B, Go.Compare, and Finance divisions.
What you'll be doing
As the Head of BI, reporting to the Director of Data & BI, you will be a senior leader within the Data & BI Leadership Team. You will own the end-to-end BI capability, acting as a trusted senior partner to the Director by translating strategic intent into a clear, executable roadmap. Leading a high-performing team of 20, you will provide the vision and professional leadership required to sustain a consultative BI function focused on impact and continuous improvement.
Key Responsibilities
Lead the BI roadmap and collaborate across the full Data & BI delivery portfolio. You will provide regular, structured updates on progress, risks, and dependencies to the Director of Data & BI and other senior stakeholders, ensuring clarity of focus and resource optimisation across all projects.
Lead the people strategy for the BI function, overseeing development, succession planning, and professional support. You will ensure your managers are equipped and supported to lead their own teams with consistency, acting as a "leader of leaders".
Translate high-level business strategy into a prioritised BI roadmap. You will work with the ELT and SLT to ensure the team's efforts are focused on high-value projects, balancing tactical needs with enterprise-wide scalability.
Experience that will put you ahead of the curve
A background in BI/Analytics leadership, within Digital Media, AdTech, or eCommerce. You understand the core concepts of audience data and digital engagement.
Experience leading teams of managers. You excel at encouraging a culture focused on impact, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
You are comfortable operating with high levels of accountability, overseeing outcomes end-to-end, and being a trusted senior partner to the Director of Data & BI.
You guide senior stakeholders toward the most impactful solutions and remain with care for Future's overall strategy. You are a communicator who values alternative perspectives while driving toward the best outcome for the business.
You bring a structured approach to managing complex delivery portfolios, identifying risks early and managing dependencies through clear communication.
While this is a leadership role, you have the technical proficiency in SQL, BigQuery, Dataform, Looker, and Looker Studio (or equivalent tools) required to guide technical strategy and ensure platform excellence.
What's in it for you
The expected range for this role is £80,000 - £95,000
This is a Hybrid role from our Bath Office, working three days from the office, two from home
… Plus more great perks, which include;
Uncapped leave, because we trust you to manage your workload and time
When we hit our targets, enjoy a share of our profits with a bonus
Refer a friend and get rewarded when they join Future
Well-being support with access to our Colleague Assistant Programmes
Opportunity to purchase shares in Future, with our Share Incentive Plan
Internal job family level T4
Who are we…
We're Future, the global leader in specialist media. With over 3,000 employees working across 200+ media brands, Future is a prime destination for passionate people worldwide looking to consume trusted, expert content that educates and inspires action - both online and off - through our specialist websites, magazines, events, newsletters, podcasts and social spaces.
We've got ambitious plans that further build on our growth momentum and unlock new opportunities – and we're looking for driven people who want to be a part of it!
Our Future, Our Responsibility - Inclusion and Diversity at Future
We embrace and celebrate diversity, making it part of who we are.
Different perspectives spark ideas, fuel creativity, and push us to innovate. That's why we're building a workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive.
When it comes to hiring, we keep it fair and inclusive, welcoming talent from every walk of life. It's not just about what you bring to the table — it's about making sure the table has room for everyone.
Because a diverse team isn't just good for business. It's the Future.
Find out more about Our Future, Our Responsibility on our website.
Please let us know if you need any reasonable adjustments made so we can give you the best experience!
#LI-Hybrid

ai_requirements_summary: A background in BI/Analytics leadership within Digital Media, AdTech, or eCommerce is essential. Candidates should have experience leading teams and a structured approach to managing complex delivery portfolios.

We are Future. The global platform for specialist media. We create expert original content that connects people to their passions across a portfolio of over 200 phenomenal brands.

title: Technicien en énergie solaire photovoltaïque
locations_alt_raw: Abidjan, Abidjan Autonomous District, Côte d'Ivoire
description_text:
Daystar Power consolide ses activités en Afrique de l’Ouest et Côte d'Ivoire et est à la recherche d’un technicien expérimenté dans le domaine du génie électrique avec une bonne connaissance en énergie solaire photovoltaïque. Le futur collaborateur aura pour mission de coordonner, réaliser, maintenir et surveiller les installations électriques solaires en Côte d'Ivoire.
Description du poste
1.Maintenance
Effectuer la maintenance de routine, préventive et corrective des installations électriques solaires et connexes.
Planifier et coordonner toutes les activités sur la centrale avec tous les intervenants, être un point focal.
Superviser/réaliser la mise en place des équipements de chargement/déchargement ainsi que ceux de sécurité.
Superviser/réaliser le remplacement des modules solaires et de racks solaires lors des interventions.
Superviser/réaliser l'installation/remplacement de câbles électriques lors des interventions.
Maintenir et sécuriser les outils d'installation et assurer un bon entretien sur le site du client.
Rédiger des rapports de maintenance et d'événements.
Résoudre les problèmes à distance ou sur site et faire le suivi des réclamations du client.
Informer l’équipe de tout problème survenant sur le site et des préoccupations des clients.
Former le client sur la façon d'exploiter et d'utiliser de manière optimale le système.
Assure la satisfaction du client 
Assurer une surveillance de la centrale sur les périodes de production à distance et à partir des plateformes fournies.
Préparer les rapports mensuels de performance.
2.Installation : coordonner et superviser les installations de solutions d'énergie solaire.
3.Réalisation d'audits énergétiques.
Education : Au minimum, un enseignement ou une formation technique et professionnelle ou un diplôme en génie électrique ou énergétique (bac +2), ou bac technique avec une expérience avérée.
Expérience : + 2 ans d'expérience professionnelle dans le domaine de la maintenance en génie électrique et énergétique, de préférence dans l’industrie des énergies renouvelables ou de l'électricité en général.
Langue : Français (courant), l’anglais parlé et écris est un avantage.
Permis de Conduire.
Compétences techniques nécessaires :
Maitrise des différentes composantes d’une centrale solaire, leur fonctionnement et leur interaction.
Une expérience dans la maintenance des équipements solaires des constructeurs SMA, SUNGROW, HUAWEI
Une maitrise des outils numériques de connections aux équipements, de collection des données historiques et des paramètres.
Une maitrise des procédures de démarrage/mise hors tension des équipements solaires
Une maitrise de l’opération et de la maintenance des cellules électriques 33kV des constructeurs Siemens, Schneider
Une maitrise des protocoles de communications entre les équipements notamment Modbus RTU, TCP
Bonnes compétences en communication et capacité à diriger et à bien travailler en équipe
Profil recherché :
Motivé(e), engagé(e) et capable de prospérer sous pression.
Très sensible au travail de qualité, esprit analytique, passion d’apprendre.
Bonnes compétences en communication et capacité à diriger et à bien travailler en équipe.
Très organisé(e) et capable de gérer plusieurs projets en parallèle et de prioriser les demandes concurrentes,
ai_core_responsibilities: The technician will coordinate, perform, maintain, and monitor solar electrical installations in Côte d'Ivoire. Responsibilities include routine maintenance, supervising installations, and ensuring client satisfaction.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least a technical education in electrical engineering or energy, with a minimum of 2 years of experience in electrical maintenance, preferably in renewable energy. Proficiency in French is required, and English is a plus.
linkedin_org_slogan: Daystar Power provides businesses in Africa with clean, continuous and affordable power.
linkedin_org_locations: Silicon Avenue, Ebene Cybercity, Mauritius 72201, MU
linkedin_org_description:
Sub-Saharan Africa is in the middle of a power crisis, with large portions of the population not having access to electricity. Businesses and consumers who do have access to the grid, often have to deal with daily power outages which can last up to several days. 

Most businesses across Sub-Saharan Africa rely on expensive and polluting diesel generators to bridge the supply-demand gap of electricity. Daystar Power focuses on hybrid solar power solutions for commercial customers for either a cash sale or a monthly fee. Daystar Power delivers three significant benefits to its customers: 1) reducing the cost of energy, 2) providing a stable power supply and  3) reducing pollution.

title: Υπεύθυνη/ος Γραμμής Παραγωγής
description_text:
Στην Αρτοβιομηχανία Καραμολέγκος πιστεύουμε ότι η ποιότητα ξεκινά από τις σωστές επιλογές. Όπως επιλέγουμε με φροντίδα τα πιο αγνά υλικά για το ψωμί μας — αλεύρι, νερό, αλάτι και προζύμι — με την ίδια προσοχή επιλέγουμε και τους ανθρώπους που βρίσκονται πίσω από κάθε μας προϊόν.
Στο πλαίσιο ενίσχυσης της ομάδας Παραγωγής, αναζητούμε Υπεύθυνη/ος Γραμμής Παραγωγής, ο/η οποίος/α θα συμβάλλει ενεργά στην ομαλή λειτουργία της παραγωγικής διαδικασίας και στη διασφάλιση της ποιότητας των προϊόντων μας.
Ως Υπεύθυνη/ος Γραμμής Παραγωγής θα έχεις την ευθύνη για:
Τον συντονισμό και την παρακολούθηση της παραγωγικής διαδικασίας στη βάρδιά σου.
Τη διασφάλιση της ορθής εφαρμογής του προγράμματος παραγωγής με έμφαση στην ποιότητα και την αποτελεσματικότητα.
Την καθοδήγηση και τον συντονισμό της ομάδας παραγωγής.
Την τήρηση των ποιοτικών προδιαγραφών, καθώς και των κανόνων υγιεινής και ασφάλειας.
Την αναγνώριση και πρόταση διορθωτικών ενεργειών για τη βελτίωση της απόδοσης και των διαδικασιών.
Τη συνεργασία με τα υπόλοιπα τμήματα για την εύρυθμη λειτουργία της παραγωγής.
Αν διαθέτεις:
Σπουδές ΤΕΙ / ΙΕΚ / ΤΕΛ / ΕΠΑΣ με κατεύθυνση μηχανολογίας, τεχνολογίας τροφίμων ή αρτοποιίας.
Τουλάχιστον 1 έτος εμπειρίας σε βιομηχανικό περιβάλλον παραγωγής.
Καλή γνώση Αγγλικών και χειρισμού Η/Υ.
Δυνατότητα εργασίας σε βάρδιες και κυλιόμενο ωράριο.
Υπευθυνότητα, οργανωτική σκέψη και διάθεση για εξέλιξη.
........θέληση, συνέπεια και διάθεση για εξέλιξη τότε θέλουμε να σε γνωρίσουμε!
Για να κάνουμε την καθημερινότητά σου καλύτερη, σου προσφέρουμε:
Ανταγωνιστικό πακέτο αποδοχών
Ιδιωτική ιατροφαρμακευτική ασφάλιση
Κάρτα διατακτικών
Ευκαιρίες επαγγελματικής εξέλιξης και ανάπτυξης
Παροχή δωρεάν προϊόντων της εταιρείας

linkedin_org_description:
KARAMOLEGOS S.A. has a great success story; starting as a small family business in Thira, Santorini in 1950. From then and on, it is driving a continuous successful route in the area of bread & bakery products.

The company enjoys today the leading position in the bread segment with a variety of favorite brands, sub-brands and products such as Karamolegos, Katselis, Deka, ‘K’, Psiha Toast, German-type bread etc.

“Karamolegos Bakery Industry SA” is active in Greece via the Company's operations and through its subsidiary, “Apollonion SA” located in Koropi Attikis. The Company also operates in Romania through its subsidiary “Karamolegos Bakery Romania SA”.

Our vision is, to offer bread creations, which add nutritional value and interest in the everyday life of the family. 

With respect to tradition, maintatining high-quality standards and above all ‘with love’, we produce every day consumers’ favorite breads. Furthermore, our products are distributed on a daily basis, all over Greece, only few hours after their production,.

“Karamolegos Bakery Industry S.A.” employs more than 450 people working with passion to ensure our consumers have the freshest and most qualitative products to feed their families at every meal.

We, employees of “Karamolegos Bakery Industry S.A.”:
Have passion for our work!
Are seeking continuous improvement!
Commit to success!
Cooperate efficiently!
Respect the effort and appreciate loyalty!

title: Software Developer
description_text:
Development, unification and renewal of the core banking system. 
Implementation of software additions that allow other internal systems and processes to report programmatically.
Preparation of the banking software for renewed banking operations and the introduction of new banking products.
Maintenance of existing databases and their compliance according to the introduction of new products.
Software development experience (3+ years) with strong problem-solving / algorithmic thinking.
Strong debugging & troubleshooting skills (ability to analyze issues end-to-end).
Database skills:
Willingness to learn and work with GT.M / MUMPS (“M”) and hierarchical, non-relational data concepts.
SQL (write/read queries, basic troubleshooting).
Willingness to learn PSL (primary language in FIS Profile).
Comfortable working with enterprise / legacy technology stacks common in banking.
Hungarian citizenship and fluent Hungarian/English language skills is a must.
Nice to have
Core Banking / Financial systems experience, especially:
Deposit products (checking/current, savings, term deposits/CDs).
Transaction processing, end-of-day / batch processing.
Clearing & settlement (including interbank flows) and high-volume transactions.
Customer data management (CIF/customer master concepts).
Awareness of regulatory / compliance expectations in financial software (auditability, controls, data handling).
Previous experience with non-relational databases.
Git (Bitbucket/GitHub workflows, branching/merging).
Unix/Linux shell scripting.
Containers: Podman (run/debug containers, basic operational troubleshooting).
You will have the opportunity to gain experience in exciting, long-term, innovative projects,
Home office option (40%/week),
Work in a multinational environment,
A team of great engineers,
Cafeteria.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves the development, unification, and renewal of the core banking system, as well as the implementation of software additions for internal systems. Additionally, it includes the maintenance of existing databases and preparation for new banking operations and products.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least 3 years of software development experience with strong problem-solving and debugging skills. Familiarity with database concepts, willingness to learn specific technologies, and fluency in Hungarian and English are also required.

title: Loader Operative
description_text:
Operative 
Location: Uckfield Depot
Pay: £12.92p/h
Shift: Monday to Friday, 07:00 
Working as part of a crew, you will be responsible for collecting and emptying food waste caddy bins across local routes on a multi-collection basis.
This role is perfect for someone who enjoys working outdoors, using their own initiative and working as part of a team.
Here’s why you’ll love it at Biffa 
On-the-job training with our in-house team
Access to Biffa Perkz – high street discounts for you and your family
Pioneering family-friendly policies 
Our Values 
We live by our values of #TFR 
Teamwork – working together for each other
Fairness – treating and being treated equally
Respect – appreciating and valuing each other for who we are and what we do
We’re here to change the way people think about waste
With over 100 years of history, Biffa’s iconic red trucks and containers are a familiar feature across the UK. Whether we’re turning waste into sustainable power, finding new ways to recycle it or simply keeping it off the streets, we believe every day is an opportunity to improve the lives of millions. Over 11,000 people around the country trust us to provide them with a career that’s always rewarding, often challenging, but never dull.
Dedicated to diversity

Being inclusive is core to our culture at Biffa; we believe different ideas, perspectives and backgrounds are key to developing a creative and effective working environment that represents our communities and generates the best outcomes for colleagues, customers and stakeholders, which is why you’ll find us championing diversity, equity and inclusion at every turn.

ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate enjoys working outdoors, can use their own initiative, and thrives in a team environment. No specific formal education or prior experience level is explicitly mentioned as a requirement.
linkedin_org_slogan: We’re here to change the way people think about waste.
linkedin_org_locations: Coronation Road, High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire HP12 3TZ, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Founded in London in 1912, Biffa is a leading UK nationwide integrated waste management business providing collection, treatment, recycling and technologically-driven energy generation services. Through the expertise of our people and investment in technology we promote and deliver sustainable waste management solutions.

We provide an essential service to satisfy the business needs of our commercial, industrial and public sector customers throughout the UK, and help them to meet their legal obligations and corporate responsibility commitments.

We employ 10,000 people and collect waste from thousands of businesses and millions of households across the UK each day.  

Our surplus food redistribution partnership with Company Shop Group, and our environmental work with BiffaAward and Waste Aid, also have wider benefits for the UK and beyond. 

Since 2002 we’ve reduced our carbon emissions by 70 per cent and aim to be net zero by 2050.

title: Junior Consultant Neonatologist-Private Healthcare Sector
description_text:
SearchPlus HR invites applications for the role of Junior Consultant Neonatologist in the private healthcare sector. This position offers a unique opportunity for those seeking to begin their career in a dynamic and well-respected environment specializing in neonatal care.
In this role, you will join a team of skilled professionals committed to providing exceptional care for newborns dealing with a variety of medical challenges. Your responsibilities will include working under the mentorship of seasoned neonatologists while collaborating with a dedicated team to ensure positive health outcomes for infants in need of specialized intervention.
Key Responsibilities:
Evaluate and manage newborns in need of intensive care, providing timely and appropriate medical interventions.
Conduct thorough physical examinations and work closely with the healthcare team to formulate effective treatment plans.
Actively participate in daily rounds and care coordination, advocating for the best interests of your patients.
Maintain open lines of communication with families regarding their child's health status and care journey.
Engage in continuous medical education and training to enhance your clinical skills and knowledge base.
Contribute to ongoing research and quality improvement initiatives within the department.
Doctor of Medicine degree with a completed residency in Pediatrics.
Fellowship in Neonatology is preferred but not a strict requirement for this junior role.
Prior clinical experience in a neonatal unit or relevant pediatric setting is advantageous.
Strong desire to learn and provide quality care to a vulnerable patient population.
Excellent teamwork and collaboration skills in a multi-disciplinary environment.
Strong verbal and written communication skills to effectively interact with patients and families.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment while maintaining composure and diligence.
A commitment to continuous professional development and evidence-based practice in neonatology.
Attention to detail and strong organizational capabilities in clinical settings.
Willingness to actively engage in clinical research and quality improvement projects.
Working Time is Full Time.
Salary is AED 45K TO AED 90K 
Benefits are Good Monthly Salary + Incentives + Standard family benefits like accommodation, Air ticket, Medical insurance, and Education allowance
ai_core_responsibilities: Evaluate and manage newborns in need of intensive care while collaborating with a healthcare team. Maintain communication with families and engage in continuous medical education.

title: Senior Brand Copywriter
description_text:
Hello, we’re Starling. We built a new kind of bank because we knew technology had the power to help people save, spend and manage their money in a new and transformative way. We’re a fully licensed UK bank with the culture and spirit of a fast-moving, disruptive tech company. We’re a bank, but better: fairer, easier to use and designed to demystify money for everyone. We employ more than 3,500 people across our London, Southampton, Cardiff and Manchester offices.
As Senior Brand Copywriter, you’ll help shape and create impactful and effective work across brand, marketing and social as we take Starling into its next chapter. 
You’ll be able to take ideas and turn them into thoughtful, crafted work that elevate the brand. It could be big campaigns with impact, or small details with finesse. You’ll be comfortable working across digital, print, motion, radio and product, and collaborate closely with designers, art directors, marketers, product designers and developers. You’ll take ownership of your work and help others raise the bar too. 
Key Responsibilities: 
Creative Execution and Writing Craft:
Deliver high-quality writing across a wide range of formats and channels.
Translate creative briefs into strong, clear concepts and executions.
Have a proven track record in scripting, concepting, editing, naming, as well as writing for OOH, radio, TV, email, web and social media.
Have a clear understanding of writing for SEO, GEO, paid search and other technical and performance marketing channels.
Take responsibility for your projects from concept to final delivery.
Bring a high level of craft, consistency and clarity to everything you create.
Present your work clearly and confidently to peers and stakeholders.

Collaboration and Team Contribution:
Work closely with writers, marketers, engineers and other designers.
Always push yourself and the team for stronger ideas and a higher level of craft in execution.
Contribute to creative reviews with ideas, creative references and positive critique.
Help maintain strong working relationships across the team and wider business.

Brand Application and Consistency:
Apply our tonal identity consistently and creatively across touchpoints.
Identify opportunities to evolve or improve how the brand is expressed.
Uphold high standards in execution, never deviating from house style.
Help ensure creative work aligns with our wider goals and brand principles

Creative Thinking and Development:
Stay aware of trends and creative work across industries.
Bring new ideas and fresh perspectives into the team.
Contribute to the culture of the creative studio with curiosity and generosity.
Take initiative in developing your own skills and craft.
5+ years of writing experience in brand, marketing or creative studio environments.
A portfolio that shows original thinking, craft and versatility.
Experience working within a brand system.
Proficiency in Figma.
Confidence in presenting your work and explaining your decisions.
Attention to detail and a collaborative mindset.
25 days holiday (plus take your public holiday allowance whenever works best for you)
An extra day’s holiday for your birthday
Annual leave is increased with length of service, and you can choose to buy or sell up to five extra days off
16 hours paid volunteering time a year
Salary sacrifice, company enhanced pension scheme
Life insurance at 4x your salary
Private Medical Insurance with VitalityHealth including mental health support and cancer care. Partner benefits include discounts with Waitrose, Mr&Mrs Smith and Peloton
Generous family-friendly policies
Perkbox membership giving access to retail discounts, a wellness platform for physical and mental health, and weekly free and boosted perks
Access to initiatives like Cycle to Work and Salary Sacrificed Gym partnerships
About Us
You may be put off applying for a role because you don't tick every box. Forget that! While we can’t accommodate every flexible working request, we're always open to discussion. So, if you're excited about working with us, but aren’t sure if you're 100% there yet, get in touch anyway.
We’re on a mission to radically reshape banking – and that starts with our brilliant team. Whatever came before, we’re proud to bring together people of all backgrounds and experiences who love working together to solve problems.
Starling Bank is an equal opportunity employer, and we’re proud of our ongoing efforts to foster diversity & inclusion in the workplace. Individuals seeking employment at Starling Bank are considered without regard to race, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, medical condition, ancestry, physical or mental disability, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.
By submitting your application, you agree that Starling Bank may collect your personal data for recruiting and related purposes. Our Privacy Notice explains what personal information we may process, where we may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over our use of your personal information.

St Peter's Square, Landmark, Manchester, England M1 4PB, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Hello, we’re Starling.

Banking was broken – so we decided to fix it. The vision? Fast technology, fair service and honest values. All at the tap of a phone, all the time.

We built Britain’s first digital bank.

One hard-won banking licence later, we set about giving people a new way to spend, save and manage their money (and take better care of the planet, too).

We’re changing banking for good.

Back then, we were obsessed with unravelling the knotty world of finance and solving people’s problems rather than selling them stuff. We still are.

Since then, we’ve grown. A lot.

Over four million accounts (and four account types!). A team of thousands. Headquartered in London with offices in Cardiff, Dublin, Manchester and Southampton. Seven years voted Which? Recommended Provider and Britain's Best Banking Brand. Still zero branches.

Our culture is open, inclusive and focused on solving real customer problems, with an emphasis on doing the right thing, even when it’s not always the easy thing. From our approach to working together and sustainability to how we build our products, our decisions need to make the world – and Starling – a better place to be.

Everyone at Starling is essential to our mission, which is really quite simple: to solve our customer’s problems – and build the best bank in the world!

And now we're providing Starling to other banks, via a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) proposition through our subsidiary Engine by Starling, using the proprietary technology platform that it uses to power our own bank.

title: Junior Consultant Periodontist-Private Sector
locations_alt_raw: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
locations_derived: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is thrilled to announce an opportunity for a Junior Consultant Periodontist to join our innovative private dental practice. In this role, you will be integral in providing high-quality periodontal care to patients, including diagnosis, treatment planning, and delivering various periodontal procedures under the guidance of senior specialists.
As a Junior Consultant, you will build your skills and gain valuable experience in managing periodontal cases while working within a dynamic and supportive team environment. We are committed to fostering your growth and development in the field of periodontics.
If you are enthusiastic about advancing your career in a progressive private sector practice and are dedicated to improving patient oral health, we encourage you to apply!

Key Responsibilities:
Assist in assessing and diagnosing periodontal issues through patient examinations and diagnostic tests.
Collaborate with senior periodontists to develop and implement treatment plans tailored to individual patient needs.
Perform basic periodontal procedures such as scaling, root planing, and assisting in more advanced surgical interventions.
Educate patients on periodontal health, treatment options, and preventive care practices.
Maintain accurate records and documentation of patient cases in compliance with healthcare regulations.
Engage in continuous professional development including training and workshops on up-to-date practices in periodontics.
Contribute to a positive and collaborative clinic environment that prioritizes patient care and satisfaction.
Support clinic operations by adhering to established protocols and policies.
Participate in team meetings and case discussions to enhance learning and collaborative decision-making.
Stay abreast of new developments in periodontology and pursue avenues for practice improvement.
Dental degree (DDS or DMD) with foundational knowledge of periodontics.
Current dental license to practice in the relevant jurisdiction.
Interest in pursuing a career in periodontology, with a focus on learning and professional growth.
Exposure to basic periodontal procedures through internships or residency programs.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills for effective patient interactions and teamwork.
Ability to learn quickly and adapt to a fast-paced clinical environment.
Detail-oriented with a commitment to providing high-quality patient care.
Proactive attitude towards professional development and skill enhancement.
Empathy and dedication to improving patient experiences and outcomes.
Willingness to collaborate and engage with senior practitioners and peers.
Benefits are Good Monthly Salary + Incentives + Standard family benefits like accommodation, Air ticket, Medical insurance, and Education allowance.
