title: Regional Head of Sales Excellence
locations_alt_raw: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description_text:
ABOUT US
As Southeast Asia’s #1 integrated logistics provider, Teleport is all about making logistics fast, affordable and reliable.
Our mission? To move things across Southeast Asia better than anyone else. We’re focused on making next-day delivery a reality for all.
#TeamTeleport is guided by our values: Move with urgency : because time is crucial; Aim to pioneer : constantly innovating to stay ahead; Do it with intent : delivering with purpose and precision; and Keep it realThat is excellent detail. I can certainly help you craft a compelling and detailed Job Description for the Regional Head of Sales Excellence at Teleport.
Location: Kuala Lumpur (Teleport HQ)
Reporting To: Regional Commercial Head
The Opportunity:
We are seeking an experienced and dynamic leader to establish and drive the Sales Excellence function across Teleport's regional footprint. This is a critical change-agent role responsible for elevating the performance, culture, and capabilities of our country-level sales teams. The ideal candidate possesses a strong background in enterprise sales, deep experience in the air transportation or logistics industry, and a proven track record in orchestrating large-scale sales transformation initiatives.Key Responsibilities:
1. Sales Strategy, Process, and Performance Elevation (Excellence Design):
Process Standardization: Design, implement, and govern world-class sales processes (from lead generation to contracting and account management) to ensure consistency, efficiency, and scalability across all markets.
Technology & Data Insights: Own the adoption and utilization of the Sales CRM and related tools. Lead data-mining and sophisticated sales analysis to identify performance gaps, untapped market opportunities, and predictive sales insights.
KPI Management: Define and manage regional Sales Excellence KPIs, focusing on pipeline health, sales cycle velocity, forecast accuracy, and adherence to best practices.
2. Cultural and Talent Transformation (Change Management):
Sales Culture Change: Drive a high-performance, customer-centric sales culture, ensuring every country sales team member embodies Teleport’s values and professional standards in their conduct, negotiation, and relationship management.
Training & Enablement: Design and deliver regional sales training programs (on-boarding, product knowledge, negotiation skills, relationship selling, and CRM usage) to elevate the technical and soft skills of the team.
Coaching & Mentoring: Work directly with Country Sales Heads to embed best practices, coach complex deal negotiation, and improve individual sales planning and execution.
3. Negotiation and Account Management Conduct:
Set the "Teleport Standard" for sales conduct, negotiation strategy, and professionalism in key account management.
Act as a senior resource and subject matter expert in complex, strategic negotiations to coach the team and ensure optimal deal structure and profitability.
Experience: A minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in B2B/Enterprise Sales, Sales Operations, or Sales Excellence roles within a high-growth, regional environment.
Industry Expertise (Preferred): Significant and demonstrable direct sales experience within the air transportation, air cargo, or freight forwarding industry is highly preferred.
Change Management Acumen: Proven experience leading or significantly contributing to large-scale organizational change management or sales transformation programs.
Analytical Skills: Expert proficiency in utilizing sales data, CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce), and business intelligence tools for deep analysis and actionable insight generation.
Leadership: Strong ability to influence, motivate, and manage stakeholders across diverse geographical and cultural teams without direct reporting authority.
Communication: Exceptional presentation, training, and written communication skills in English. Proficiency in a key Southeast Asian language is a plus.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Commercial Management, or a related field. An advanced degree is a benefit.

title: Software & Performance Engineer, Fintech
description_text:
Optasia is a fully enabled B2B2X financial technology platform covering scoring, financial decisioning, disbursement and collection. We are committed to enabling financial inclusion for all. We are changing the world our way.
We are seeking for enthusiastic professionals, with energy, who are results driven and have can-do attitude, who want to be part of a team of likeminded individuals who are delivering solutions in an innovative and exciting environment. As a Software & Performance Engineer, you will be pivotal in the development of software that provides real solutions. You will be deeply involved in the entire software development lifecycle and an integral part of all procedures to design, code, analyze and document application programs in Spring Framework. Working closely with other teams, you will be actively contributing to the process of evolving the company's products and you will play an important role in identifying and applying appropriate modern technologies and techniques for software development. 
What you will do
Monitor and support production environments
Collaborate with other engineers to design, implement and publish to the world, demanding fintech services using Spring Framework
Identify and apply appropriate modern technologies and techniques for software development
Perform load-tests, analyze results, find root cause of performance issues and provide appropriate solutions proactively
Troubleshoot challenging performance and stability issues in real world environments 
What you will bring
At least 5 years' of experience in a similar role
Passion about software development
Passion about application performance, reliability and scalability
Strong software development skills in Java, Spring and bash scripting
Solid foundation regarding computer science fundamentals including systems and networks
Experience with distributed high concurrency and high throughput applications
Experience and understanding of Relational databases, modeling and tuning for high throughput applications (Postgres)
Experience and understanding of load balancers and reverse proxies (httpd, nginx)
Experience with NoSQL databases (Elastic, Cassandra, Mongo) will be considered a plus
Experience with queueing and event streaming systems
Knowledge of using, configuring and setup of container orchestration tools (Docker Swarm, Kubernetes) will be considered a plus
Familiarity with CI/CD tools would be considered a plus 
Experience in working with secure code development guidelines and coding practices (i.e. OWASP, NIST)
Your key attributes
Strong desire for exploring, evaluating and understanding in depth old and new technologies
Passion for learning new technologies and eagerness to collaborate with other creative minds
Excellent judgment, organizational and problem-solving skills
Ability to hit tight deadlines and work under pressure and strict attention to detail
Excellent communication skills and team working mentality 
Why you should apply
What we offer:
💸 Competitive remuneration package
🏝 Extra day off on your birthday
💰 Performance-based bonus scheme
👩🏽‍⚕️ Comprehensive private healthcare insurance
📲 💻 All the tech gear you need to work smart
Optasia’s Perks:
🎌 Be a part of a multicultural working environment
🎯 Meet a very unique and promising business and industry
🌌 🌠 Gain insights for tomorrow market’s foreground
🎓 A solid career path within our working family is ready for you
📚 Continuous training and access to online training platforms
🥳 CSR activities and festive events within any possible occasion
🍜 Enjoy comfortable open space restaurant with varied meal options every day
🎾 🧘‍️ Wellbeing activities access such as free on-site yoga classes, plus available squash court on our premises
Optasia’s Values 🌟
#1 Drive to Thrive: Fully dedicated to evolving. We welcome all challenges and learning opportunities.
#2 Customer-First Mindset: We go above and beyond to meet our partners’ and clients’ expectations.
#3 Bridge the Gap: Knowledge is shared, information is exchanged and every opinion counts.
#4 Go-Getter Spirit: We are results oriented. We identify any shortcomings that hold us back and step up to do what’s needed.
#5 Together we will do it: We are committed to supporting one another and to understanding and respecting different perspectives, as we aim to reach our common goals.
ai_benefits: Extra day off on your birthday
All the tech gear you need to work smart
Continuous training and access to online training platforms

Key tasks include monitoring production environments, collaborating on demanding fintech services, and proactively identifying and resolving performance and stability issues.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least 5 years of experience with strong skills in Java, Spring, and bash scripting, alongside a solid foundation in computer science fundamentals, systems, and networks. Experience with high concurrency applications, relational databases like Postgres, and familiarity with load balancers, NoSQL databases, and container orchestration tools are required or considered a plus.
linkedin_org_description:
We deliver Optasia, the AI Platform enabling financial access for the next billion customers. We are changing the world our way.

Α versatile, data-driven B2B2X (business-to-business-to-customers and SMEs) financial technology platform for MNOs, mobile wallet operators and financial institutions, which provides them with accurate credit scoring for responsible financing decision-making. 

We support intelligent credit decisions, enable a broad range of financial services development and drive successful business models. All with ease, through our AI-led fully-automated decisioning algorithm, which allows us to efficiently make millions of decisions on financing, based on sophisticated data analysis.

Thanks to our technology and network we provide powerful flywheel effects with repeatable and scalable growth levers, to help our partners grow and enable financial access to retail & SME customers across numerous markets.

Operating since 2012 and expanded in 38 countries (as of October 2025) in Africa, Middle East, Asia and Europe with focus in emerging markets, Optasia is enabling credit decisions for an average of over 120 million customers a month and had more than 860 million addressable subscribers in 2024, continuing to expand to new markets globally.

Optasia has been chosen as an investment opportunity by top firms such as Abu Dhabi's Waha Capital in 2017, and Ethos, a leading South African investment company in late 2018. The company also obtained ISO 27001 Certification in late 2021. Founded as Channel VAS in 2012, we became Optasia in 2022, incorporating a brand identity that more broadly reflects our capabilities.

title: ADHD Operations Manager
description_text:
ADHD Operations Manager 
On site | London (NW1) | 12-Month FTC
£64,000 - £68,000 per annum (DOE)
Closing Date: Monday 16th February 2026 
The job ad may be closed sooner
Help Shape the Future of ADHD Care across England
Healthcare Central London (HCL) is launching a brand-new Adult ADHD Diagnosis & Treatment Service under NHS Right to Choose and we are looking for an exceptional Operations Manager to help mobilise, lead and grow this national service.
This is more than an operations role.
This is an opportunity to build something meaningful, lead multidisciplinary teams, influence national care delivery and directly improve patient outcomes.
About Us
Healthcare Central London (HCL) is a federation of 30 GP practices and 4 PCNs in Westminster, delivering innovative, community-led NHS services to over 275,000 patients. We are known for our pioneering “Octopus” care model and our strong commitment to tackling health inequalities.
The Opportunity
You will provide operational leadership for HCL’s Adult ADHD Service, working closely with clinical leaders to ensure safe, compliant, high-quality and efficient care delivery across multiple ICBs nationwide.
You will lead teams, manage performance, mobilise services, strengthen governance and help shape the future of digital mental health services.
Hybrid working: Hybrid- Capital House (Up to 2 days working from home). There will be an expectation for some out of hours work and ‘on-call’ support especially the mobilisation phase.
Key Responsibilities
• Lead and develop the ADHD operational team
• Manage service mobilisation and day-to-day operations
• Ensure CQC compliance, governance and quality standards
• Oversee performance, rotas, reporting and SLAs
• Work with ICBs, finance, BI and digital transformation teams
• Drive service improvement and innovation
• Lead recruitment, appraisal and performance management
• Manage complaints, audits and service development
About You
You will be an experienced healthcare operations leader with:
• Senior operational management experience
• Mental health / ADHD service experience
• Proven leadership of multidisciplinary teams
• Governance, CQC and quality improvement knowledge
• Confidence managing hybrid teams and national contracts
• Passion for innovation and service development
• Experience with mobilising new services
Why Join HCL?
Build and shape a national ADHD service
Hybrid working model
Competitive salary
Strong values-led culture
Opportunity to influence real patient impact
Career-defining leadership opportunity
Ready to Apply?
If you are driven by purpose, innovation and excellence in care delivery, we’d love to hear from you.
Apply now and be part of shaping the future of ADHD services in England.
Apply by: Monday 16th February
ai_core_responsibilities: You will provide operational leadership for HCL’s Adult ADHD Service, ensuring safe and efficient care delivery. Key responsibilities include managing service mobilisation, overseeing performance, and driving service improvement.
ai_requirements_summary: You will need senior operational management experience, particularly in mental health or ADHD services. Proven leadership skills and knowledge of governance and quality improvement are essential.

locations_alt_raw: Newcastle upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
locations_derived: Newcastle upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
description_text:
Beauty Retail Artist, H Beauty - Newcastle
Part-time position (24hours), Fixed-term Contract until August 2026. 
About the Charlotte Tilbury Brand
Founded by British makeup artist, Charlotte Tilbury MBE, in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de-coding makeup application for every person, at any age, with an easy-to-use, easy-to-choose, easy-to-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty, continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories. 
A Charlotte Tilbury Retail Artist is the true heart of where all the magic happens in our stores and counters. Based on the shop floor, you are responsible for encompassing our mission of empowering our customers to be the most beautiful version of themselves, so they have the confidence to achieve their dreams. You do this through combining your expert artistry skills with exceptional product knowledge and a passion for building lasting emotional connections. You believe in the product being accessible to everybody – and you can adapt your style to suit the needs of your customer.
You can work at a fast pace to achieve and exceed KPI’s. You feed on the buzz of achieving your goals and being the best, you can be. You not only meet expectations – you exceed them.
“Teamwork makes the dream work” – is your working motto. You build solid relationships with team members and management contributing to a sense of team work in all aspects of the role.
You champion the art of world-class artistry and an impeccable customer experience. You are a Charlotte Tilbury brand ambassador and want to pass on your knowledge to customer after customer. 
You easily adapt your approach to activities, customers and opportunities to open up for stratospheric success.
You follow the company grooming standards to a tee – you know what good looks like and are not afraid to display this.
You will have
Ideally an NVQ-3 in Beauty Therapy 
You will ideally have had some experience as a Sales Advisor. Ideally this will be gained from a fast-paced environment such as beauty, retail or FMCG.
You aspire to be part of a beauty brand that disrupts the way it is seen – and continues to grow. You love all things Charlotte Tilbury – and are not afraid to spread the word.
Ideally you will have experience in applying makeup – and knowing how to adapt your style to different customers with different needs.
You can only be the best when you are set up to succeed – not only do we offer on the job training. We offer you the opportunity to attend Charlotte’s Magic Academy – where dreams are truly made. Combining art, commerce and Charlotte’s own values. You will feel truly empowered to live the magic in your role.
Opportunities to disrupt processes (in a positive way!) and have your voice truly heard.
The opportunity to be part of a company where we celebrate our achievements, and you are included. With a real scope for growth and progression to support your own development.
You receive exclusive launches of our incredible products before anyone else – not only that you even receive a full Charlotte Tilbury look once you pass your probation. (That is right all the products from one of our iconic looks)
We also offer an incredible staff discount, and access to Tilbury Treats – our very own rewards platform allowing you to save money and gain money cannot buy discounts on anything from gym memberships to cinema tickets.
At Charlotte Tilbury, our mission is to empower everybody in the world to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We support this by encouraging and hiring people with diverse backgrounds, voices, beliefs, and perspectives into our growing global workforce. By doing so, we better serve our communities, customers, employees - and the candidates that take part in our recruitment process.
WE WOULD LOVE TO WELCOME YOU TO OUR DREAM TEAM…APPLY TODAY!

title: Senior Software Developer- Sad Ven
description_text:
Aspire Software is looking for a Senior Software Developer to join our team in Lebanon.
Here is a little window into our company: Aspire Software operates and manages wholly owned software companies, providing mission-critical solutions across multiple verticals. By implementing industry best practices, Aspire delivers a time sensitive integration process, and the operation of a decentralized model has allowed it to become a hub for creating rapid growth by reinvesting in its portfolio.
About the Role :
We’re building innovative AI-powered call receptionist solutions tailored for the restaurant industry. We are looking for a Staff Software Engineer who will set the technical bar for the product and the team building it. You’ll lead by example: writing code, making hard architectural calls, coaching engineers, and helping us scale without slowing down. You’ll be helping multiple teams move faster without breaking things and making sure the system we’re building today still works six months from now. 
Key Responsibilities 
Set technical direction 
Shape the overall architecture across frontend, backend, and integrations. 
Make and document clear technical decisions, especially where trade-offs matter. 
Help teams think through scaling, reliability, and complexity before problems show up in production. 
Be the person engineers turn to when problems are hard and the answer isn’t obvious. 
Enable and level-up the teams 
Work across teams, helping engineers grow their skills and confidence. 
Coach through real work: pairing, reviewing code, and discussing design decisions. 
Raise the bar for what “good” looks like, while keeping things pragmatic and shippable. 
Create space for teams to move faster by removing technical and process friction. 
Improve how we build and ship 
Establish and evolve best practices around: 
Testing (unit, integration, end-to-end) 
Release and deployment workflows 
Code quality and review standards 
Help us ship frequently and safely, without slowing teams down. 
Introduce tools, automation, and processes only when they clearly add value. 
Stay hands-on where it counts 
Contribute directly to the codebase, especially in high-impact or complex areas. 
Jump in to unblock teams, prototype solutions, or stabilize critical systems. 
Lead by example through the quality of your code and technical judgment. 
Experience operating at a senior or staff engineer level on real production systems. 
A track record of influencing multiple engineers or teams through technical leadership. 
Strong JavaScript / TypeScript experience across frontend and backend. 
Experience designing and maintaining external integrations or APIs. 
Comfort making architectural trade-offs in environments that are still evolving. 
Experience using AI-assisted coding tools to move faster, and you’re opinionated about where they help, where they don’t, and how to use them responsibly in a production codebase. 
You’re comfortable with 
Ambiguity - not everything is figured out yet, and that’s part of the job. 
Balancing speed with long-term quality. 
Giving and receiving direct, thoughtful feedback. 
Being accountable for outcomes, not just implementations. 
Preferred Skills and Qualifications:
Experience with Voice AI, conversational systems, or real-time applications. 
Experience with POS systems, booking platforms, or complex SaaS integrations. 
Time spent in early-stage or scaling startups.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Software Developer will set the technical direction by shaping the overall architecture across frontend, backend, and integrations, making critical technical decisions. They will also be responsible for enabling and leveling up engineering teams through coaching, code reviews, and raising quality standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have experience operating at a senior or staff engineer level on real production systems, demonstrating a track record of influencing multiple teams through technical leadership. Strong proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript across frontend and backend, experience designing integrations/APIs, and comfort with architectural trade-offs in evolving environments are required.

title: Junior Specialist in Pediatric Dentistry

title: Senior Brand Acquisition & Partnerships Manager
locations_alt_raw: New York, New York, United States
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
Wolf & Badger is on a mission to make retail fair. Working to support our community of 2000+ independent designers from around the globe, we connect customers with ethically sourced fashion, design, home and wellness through our online marketplace and flagship stores in London, New York City and Los Angeles. We are a rapidly growing scale-up business, now entering an exciting new phase of growth and looking to expand our team of c.110 people currently located between London, NY & LA. We are a Certified B Corporation, with social and environmental purpose at the core of everything we do, read more about us.
We are looking for a bold and results-driven Senior Brand Acquisition & Partnerships Manager to lead our commercial acquisition strategy for the US market, reporting directly to the Head of Brands. The US represents our largest and highest-potential geography for brand partnerships, and this is a pivotal senior leadership role sitting at the heart of our growth strategy, combining sophisticated pipeline management with P&L ownership and market leadership. You will have significant autonomy to shape how we identify, engage, and partner with exceptional independent brands across fashion, beauty, home, and jewelry categories, whilst directly managing the US-based brands team, currently comprising an Associate and a Manager in charge of Success.
The Senior Brand Acquisition & Partnerships Manager will thrive in a fast-paced environment, bring sophisticated sales expertise at scale, and be capable of taking full ownership of high-impact acquisition initiatives in a complex two-sided marketplace. At Wolf & Badger we champion a low-ego environment where cross-functional debate is encouraged in order to reach the best outcomes. We strive to be mission-orientated and fair at all times, and the Senior Brand Acquisition & Partnerships Manager will join us in growing an engaged, forward-thinking and high-functioning team.
Key Responsibilities & Accountabilities
Lead and own the end-to-end acquisition strategy and execution for the US market, designing data-driven approaches that drive consistent, ambitious growth in new brand partnerships
Manage and optimize a sophisticated pipeline of 100-120+ active prospects in HubSpot, conducting 8-10 qualified brand conversations daily through consultative, value-led sales approach that reflects our mission and values
Shape US category mix and brand selection criteria, identifying strategic opportunities in fashion, beauty, home, and jewelry that align with our B Corp values and drive sustainable revenue growth
Build and strengthen relationships with high-value and hero brands, leading strategic partnership development that elevates Wolf & Badger's market position in the independent retail space
Manage, develop, and empower the US-based brands team (currently an Associate and a Success Manager), ensuring seamless acquisition-to-retention handoff and designing processes that maximize brand satisfaction and long-term partnership value
Provide leadership, coaching, and performance management for direct reports, fostering a high-performing team culture aligned with Wolf & Badger's mission and values
Lead US market insights and competitive intelligence, tracking performance against ambitious targets and providing regular strategic updates to senior leadership on market trends, category opportunities, and acquisition performance
Represent Wolf & Badger at industry events, trade shows, and networking opportunities, building strategic relationships in the US independent brand community
Ensure we maintain our position as a leading ethical, B Corp certified, retailer by considering sustainability, fairness, and social impact at all points in the acquisition process
Required:
Proven leadership in B2B sales or partnerships with a strong commercial mindset and hands-on approach, ideally in marketplaces, SaaS, fashion/retail tech, or related industries
Experience managing and developing high-performing teams, with a track record of coaching, mentoring, and driving team performance in fast-paced environments
Extensive experience managing complex, consultative sales cycles with 5-7+ years track record of consistently exceeding revenue targets in senior IC or leadership roles
Advanced expertise in CRM and pipeline management, with strong preference for HubSpot proficiency and data-driven decision making capabilities
A track record of successfully building relationships with founders, creative directors, and brand decision-makers, with natural ability to connect mission-driven independent businesses with growth opportunities
A strategic and analytical mindset, always seeking smarter, faster, and more cost-effective solutions while maintaining quality and mission alignment
Exceptional communication skills, enabling you to collaborate at all levels of the organisation and represent Wolf & Badger's values authentically
Self-directed and thrives working autonomously with high accountability, comfortable operating across timezones with UK-based leadership and global teams
Energy, humour, and self-awareness, thriving in a feedback-driven, growth-oriented culture, enabling everyone to grow and learn
Highly Desired:
HubSpot experience
Marketplace or two-sided platform experience
Understanding of the independent brand landscape (fashion, beauty, home, jewelry)
Experience with US e-commerce market dynamics and cross-border trade
Familiarity with sustainability-focused or B Corp businesses
Previous experience in mission-driven or values-based organizations
This is more than just a sales role - it is an opportunity to drive transformation in how independent brands reach conscious consumers globally. If you're an ambitious leader ready to make an impact at the intersection of commerce, creativity, and sustainability, we'd love to hear from you!
Competitive compensation. $80-$100k base salary + performance-based commission totalling an anticipated OTE of circa $160k+
Comprehensive health benefits: Medical, dental, and vision insurance
Generous time off: Paid vacation, sick leave, and company holidays, plus paid volunteering leave
Flexible hybrid working: Work from our creative New York office with hybrid flexibility (3+ days per week onsite)
Professional development: Excellent career opportunities in a rapidly growing international business
Wellness support: Quarterly Wellness Wallet to spend on products or services meaningful to you
Staff discount: Across Wolf & Badger brands, in-store and online
Mission-driven work: Work in a positive organisation for people and the planet. We are a certified B Corp!
Wolf & Badger is an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates. We encourage applications from people from all walks of life.

title: Senior Software Engineer- Azure App Service
description_text:
This role is for one of the Weekday's clients
Salary range: Rs 1000000 - Rs 2500000 (ie INR 10-25 LPA)

Min Experience: 6 years
Location: Chennai
JobType: full-time

We are seeking an experienced Senior Software Engineer with strong expertise in C#, .NET Core Web API, Angular, and Microsoft Azure. In this role, you will design, build, and deploy scalable, cloud-native applications using Azure App Service, while collaborating closely with cross-functional teams and mentoring junior engineers. You will play a key role in developing robust APIs, driving best practices, and ensuring high performance and reliability across the application lifecycle.
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and maintain RESTful Web APIs using C# and .NET Core. 
Build and enhance modern frontend applications using Angular and JavaScript/TypeScript. 
Deploy, configure, monitor, and optimize applications on Azure App Service and related Azure services. 
Implement secure, scalable, and high-performance solutions aligned with business needs. 
Work with relational databases such as SQL Server, Azure SQL, and PostgreSQL. 
Set up and manage CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions. 
Collaborate with product managers, architects, and stakeholders to deliver end-to-end solutions. 
Conduct code reviews, enforce coding standards, and mentor junior developers. 
Troubleshoot production issues and improve application performance and reliability. 
Stay current with emerging trends in .NET, Azure, and cloud-native development. 
Required SkillsMust-Have
Strong proficiency in C# and .NET Core (Web API). 
Hands-on experience with Azure App Service, App Service Plans, and Azure Monitoring. 
Frontend development experience using Angular. 
Solid understanding of JavaScript and TypeScript. 
Experience with SQL Server / Azure SQL / PostgreSQL. 
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps preferred). 
Strong understanding of REST APIs, application security, and authentication mechanisms (OAuth, JWT). 
Good-to-Have
Experience with Azure Functions, Service Bus, or Storage Accounts. 
Knowledge of Docker and containerization. 
Exposure to microservices architecture. 
Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies. 
Azure certifications are a plus. 
Eligibility & Qualifications
Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. 
Experience: 6–9 years of relevant software development experience. 
Other Details
Notice Period: 30 days 
Work Type: Full-time
ai_core_responsibilities: The Senior Software Engineer will design, develop, and maintain RESTful Web APIs and modern frontend applications while deploying and optimizing applications on Azure App Service. They will also mentor junior developers and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver end-to-end solutions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have strong proficiency in C# and .NET Core, along with hands-on experience in Azure App Service and frontend development using Angular. A solid understanding of REST APIs and experience with CI/CD pipelines are also required.
linkedin_org_description: We are a YC-backed recruitment startup. Find select jobs posted by premium YC as well as VC backed startups here. Hand-curated by Weekday team.

title: Expert Public Sector Partnerships
description_text:
Position Title: Expert Public Sector Partnerships
Location: Islamabad
Grade Level: L2
Last Date to Apply : 14th February 2026
What is Expert Public Sector Partnerships?
We are seeking a highly motivated, detail-oriented, and cross-functional resource to manage B2G Analytics & Growth for our Public Sector Partnerships vertical. This role plays a critical part in enabling the digitization of public finance, designing innovative financial solutions for government and INGO partners and delivering insights that drive strategic decisions. The ideal candidate will have strong technical acumen, government engagement experience, and the ability to align internal teams to deliver high-impact digital financial services at scale. This position reports directly to the Manager B2G Solutions, Reporting & Growth of Public Sector Partnerships.
What does Expert Public Sector Partnerships do?
1. Strategic Planning & Long-Term Roadmap
Co-lead quarterly, annual, and 5-year strategic planning for the B2G vertical in collaboration with internal leadership and ecosystem partners.
Develop business cases, financial models, and performance forecasts to guide vertical prioritization and onboarding decisions.
Identify new use cases and growth opportunities across federal, provincial, and development sector institutions.
Ensure alignment of strategic roadmap with State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) policies and national financial inclusion objectives.
2. Growth, Pricing & Campaign Management
Lead design and execution of growth strategies across government verticals including in close coordination with the regional teams: 
Price revisions based on partner segmentation, product performance, and market benchmarks.
New vertical identification to diversify revenue streams.
End-user engagement campaigns: digital awareness, onboarding incentives, and usage boosts tailored to beneficiaries in coordination with relevant product and marketing teams
Partner engagement campaigns: develop digital and on-ground activation strategies to deepen partner involvement and visibility in coordination with relevant account managers and trade marketing teams
Collaborate with Marketing, Product, and Field Ops teams to deliver campaigns across all touchpoints.
3. Executive Presentations & Strategic Communication
Prepare and deliver high-quality presentation decks for internal reviews, ELT meetings, and partner engagements.
Present performance summaries, strategic proposals, and regulatory updates to JazzCash leadership and public sector stakeholders.
Translate complex data and solution architecture into clear, actionable insights for decision-makers.
4. Solution Design & Proposal Development
Design and propose digital payment solutions tailored for large government organizations and autonomous bodies, for digital financial inclusion and solutions
Ensure that all proposed solutions are scalable, compliant, and integrated with JazzCash’s core offerings.
JazzCash is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate, support, and thrive on diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
Requirements
What are we looking for and what does it require to be Expert Public Sector Partnerships?
Qualifications & Requirements
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Economics, Public Policy, or a related field. Technical background is a plus.
2-3 years of experience in public sector engagement, fintech, digital banking, or enterprise solutioning.
Demonstrated experience in performance analytics, operational process design, and stakeholder management. Excellence in data analysis and visualization tools is required. Excellence in presentation tools is required
Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with ability to engage C-level stakeholders.
Advanced proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, and reporting tools (e.g., MSTR, Power BI, Tableau).
What Success Looks Like
Excellent and actionable insights and performance management 
Partner satisfaction and growth in revenue/transaction volume across key government accounts by identifying digital financial solutions. 
Proactive identification of new verticals that can contribute to B2G growth.
Short-, medium- and long-term strategy for B2G department 
Why Join JazzCash?
As one of the largest digital financial services providers in Pakistan, our objective is to continue to change the lives of our 57 million customers for the better.
Recognized as one of the leading employers in the country, JazzCash epitomizes the philosophy that each JazzCash employee is passionately living a better life every day, inspired and enabled by visionary leadership, a unique professional culture, a flourishing lifestyle, and continuous learning and development.
Our core values include qualities essential for a positive organizational culture - truthfully guiding entrepreneurial and innovative mindsets, harnessing professional and interpersonal collaboration, and fostering across-the-board customer obsession.
This is an opportunity for someone who wants to be part of something transformative, someone who can play a critical role in driving our success. Together, we can empower millions more with the tools necessary to progress in an increasingly digital economy.
ai_core_responsibilities: This role involves co-leading strategic planning for the B2G vertical, developing business cases, and identifying new growth opportunities across government institutions. Key duties also include leading growth strategies, managing pricing, executing engagement campaigns, and preparing high-quality presentations for leadership and stakeholders.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Economics, Public Policy, or a related field, coupled with 2-3 years of experience in public sector engagement, fintech, or enterprise solutioning. Required competencies include strong data analysis, visualization, presentation skills, and advanced proficiency in tools like Excel and PowerPoint.
linkedin_org_slogan: Empowering Pakistan through digital financial services since 2012
linkedin_org_locations: JazzCash HQ, 1-A Kohistan Road, F-8, Islamabad, Islamabad Capital Territory , PK
linkedin_org_description:
JazzCash is Pakistan’s leading digital financial services provider. We offer a broad portfolio of branchless banking services for customers, including travel, ticketing, entertainment, money transfer, bill payments, debit card, insurance, savings, and payments for a wide variety of services. 

JazzCash has been a game-changer in the mobile financial services industry in Pakistan. By offering a range of financial services and an extensive network of agents and partner banks, it has made it easier for millions of previously unbanked or underbanked individuals to access financial services and participate in the formal economy.

title: Customer Service Advisor
description_text:
🌟 Join Our Team at First Mile - Where Recycling Meets Excellence! 🌟
Are you ready to embark on a thrilling journey with one of the UK's most dynamic environmental champions? Look no further! First Mile is not just a recycling company; we're trailblazers in sustainability, setting the bar high since 2004. With over 30,000 business customers relying on us to revolutionise their recycling practices, we're on a mission to redefine the way the world views waste.
🌍 **About First Mile:**
At First Mile, we're not just about recycling; we're about making a difference. Our vibrant culture celebrates individuality, fosters creativity, and champions impact. Join us in our quest to safeguard the planet, one recycled item at a time!
🚀 **About The Role: Customer Services Executive**
If you're passionate about delivering top-notch customer service, we have the perfect role for you! As a Customer Services Executive, you'll be at the forefront of our mission, dazzling customers with lightning-fast responses and expert solutions. From taking orders to tackling recycling queries, no challenge is too big for you!
⏰ **Working Hours & Compensation:**
- Monday to Friday, 8:00-4:00/ 9:00-5.00 with a 30 minute lunch break 
- Starting salary: £24,650 per year, plus an enticing performance bonus 
🌟 **Why Join Us?**
At First Mile, we're not just a company; we're a family united by a common goal: to make a meaningful impact on the world. Join us in the heart of Birmingham and be part of something bigger – a rapidly growing, multi-award-winning business where your voice matters, your growth is prioritised, and your passion for the planet drives everything we do.
Ready to take the leap? Apply now and let's embark on this exciting journey together! 🌱🚀
Responsibilities
- Handling inbound calls and emails from our valued customers.
- Resolving queries with finesse, whether routine or complex.
- Mastering the art of complaint handling like a true pro.
A sparkling phone manner and a can-do attitude.
- Proactivity and enthusiasm that shines through every interaction.
🎁 **Perks & Benefits:**
- Continuous learning and development opportunities.
- Clear pathways for career (and salary) progression within our dynamic team.
- Enhanced holiday allowance starting at 25 days plus bank holidays
-a monthly £60 personal allowance to spend on you.
- Regular social events and celebrations to keep the fun alive!
- Enhanced parental leave scheme because family matters.
- Hybrid working available after probation period.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Customer Service Advisor will handle inbound calls and emails from customers, resolving queries and managing complaints. They are expected to provide expert solutions and maintain a positive attitude in all interactions.
ai_requirements_summary: The job does not specify particular educational or experience requirements, but emphasizes the need for a sparkling phone manner and a can-do attitude. Proactivity and enthusiasm are also highlighted as important traits.
linkedin_org_slogan:
Helping over 30,000 UK businesses reduce their carbon impact with our range of recycling services.

B Corp Certified.
linkedin_org_locations: 70-71 Wells Street, London, England W1T 3QE, GB
linkedin_org_description:
First Mile are one of London's greenest recycling & waste management companies, helping over 30,000 businesses reduce their carbon impact with a range of recycling & commercial waste services.

We’re the first waste management company in London to become B Corp Certified.

Our tailored approach to recycling & waste management offers reliable & flexible rubbish collection, waste removal, & recycling services at competitive prices with 24/7 support. Our rubbish removal & waste management services range from zero-to-landfill general waste & recycling to specialist recycling services such as confidential waste & secure shredding.

Join over 30,000 London businesses reducing their carbon impact with our reliable waste collections, 24/7 support & a range of rubbish recycling services.

locations_alt_raw: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
locations_derived: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is an esteemed human resources consulting firm dedicated to pairing exceptional healthcare professionals with superior institutions. We are currently seeking a talented Senior Consultant in Orthopedic Surgery to join our esteemed client facilities. In this critical role, you will be at the forefront of orthopedic care, diagnosing, treating, and managing complex musculoskeletal conditions with both surgical and non-surgical approaches.
Your expertise will be invaluable as you work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams to ensure optimal patient outcomes. Additionally, you will have the opportunity to mentor junior staff, participate in surgical training programs, and contribute to the continuous improvement of orthopedic practices.
Requirements:
Medical degree with a specialization in Orthopedic Surgery and valid certification.
Significant experience in orthopedic surgery with a proven track record in performing complex surgical procedures.
In-depth knowledge of contemporary orthopedic techniques, technologies, and best practices.
Demonstrated leadership skills with the ability to mentor and motivate surgical teams.
Excellent communication skills to effectively interact with patients, families, and healthcare professionals.
Strong critical thinking and problem-solving abilities for comprehensive patient assessment and treatment planning.
Detail-oriented and organized with expertise in managing multiple cases and priorities.
A commitment to ongoing education and professional development in the field of orthopedic surgery.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus

title: Junior Specialist Oral and Dental Surgeon
description_text:
About SearchPlus HR
SearchPlus HR specializes in recruiting top-tier healthcare talent for leading medical institutions. We are currently seeking a Junior Specialist Oral and Dental Surgeon to join our esteemed network of healthcare facilities, where you will embark on an exciting career in oral and dental surgery.
Position Overview
The Junior Specialist Oral and Dental Surgeon will work alongside experienced dental professionals, providing vital support in diagnosing and treating various oral health conditions. This position is a fantastic opportunity for recent graduates or early-career professionals looking to enhance their surgical skills in a supportive environment.
Key Responsibilities
Assist in patient evaluations to identify oral surgical needs and develop treatment plans.
Participate in surgical procedures under the supervision of senior dental surgeons.
Provide post-operative care to ensure successful recovery and address patient concerns.
Collaborate with dental teams to provide comprehensive care and enhance treatment protocols.
Engage in continuous learning opportunities to improve surgical techniques and knowledge.
Educate patients about dental care, aftercare, and preventive measures to promote oral health.
Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) or Doctor of Medicine in Dentistry (DMD).
Completion of a residency program in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery or a related field.
Active license or eligibility for licensure to practice in the relevant jurisdiction.
Basic exposure to surgical procedures in an academic or clinical setting.
Strong understanding of oral anatomy and surgical principles.
Excellent communication skills, with an ability to interact effectively with patients and healthcare teams.
Eagerness to learn and develop skills in a fast-paced medical environment.
Team-oriented mindset with a commitment to providing exceptional patient care.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus
ai_core_responsibilities: The Junior Specialist Oral and Dental Surgeon will assist in patient evaluations and participate in surgical procedures under supervision. They will also provide post-operative care and collaborate with dental teams to enhance treatment protocols.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) or Doctor of Medicine in Dentistry (DMD) and complete a residency program in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery. They should also have basic exposure to surgical procedures and strong communication skills.

title: Housekeeping Supervisor in new project in Milos island
description_text:
Housekeeping Supervisor
Location: Milos Island, Greece
SWOT Hospitality Management Company is excited to announce the launch of a new project on the beautiful Milos Island, and we are looking for a passionate and experienced
Housekeeping Supervisor
to lead our housekeeping team in delivering exceptional service and maintaining the pristine standards of our property.
As the Housekeeping Supervisor, you will play a crucial role in setting the tone for an outstanding guest experience. Your leadership will ensure that our guests enjoy a meticulously clean and comfortable environment during their stay. You will work closely with management to maintain standards while fostering a culture of teamwork and excellence among the staff.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
· Oversee the daily operations of the housekeeping department.
· Ensure the cleanliness and presentation of guest rooms, public spaces, and back-of-house areas.
· Train and mentor housekeeping staff in best practices and company standards.
· Manage inventory for cleaning supplies and equipment.

title: Freelance Automotive Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design graduate- and industry-level automotive engineering problems grounded in real practice; 
Evaluate AI-generated solutions for correctness, assumptions, and engineering logic; 
Validate analytical or numerical results using Python (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with first principles and accepted engineering standards; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to assess multi-step problem solving. 
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for automotive engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Automotive Engineering or related fields, e.g. Mechatronics, Manufacturing Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, etc. 
3+ years of professional automotive engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, PMP) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $12/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a degree in Automotive Engineering or related fields and at least 3 years of professional experience. Strong proficiency in Python and written English is required, along with a stable internet connection.

title: AI & NLP Engineer
description_text:
At Uni Systems, we are working towards turning digital visions into reality. We are continuously growing and we are looking for a professional AI & NLP Engineer to join our UniQue Brussels, Belgium team.
What will you be bringing to the team?
Development and maintenance of software applications in the field of Natural Language Processing (NLP), Machine Learning (ML) and/or Artificial Intelligence (AI); 
Training of custom machine learning / deep learning models based on structured and unstructured data; 
Selecting features, building and optimizing classifiers using machine learning techniques; 
Studies and developments aiming at improving the quality of machine translation (MT) engines for each installed language pair, addressing the specific needs of customers of the service concerning MT quality and contributing to a general strategy for the systematic evaluation and long-term improvement of MT quality; 
Interact with data stewards and other IT stakeholders to define the data rules; 
Define data controls and implement actions to ensure data quality and integrity; 
Creating automated anomaly detection systems and constant tracking of its performance; 
Data mining using state-of-the-art methods; 
Processing, cleansing, and verifying the integrity of data used for analysis; 
Design the IT architecture for solitons in the NLP / ML / AI fields, and coordinate its implementation considering master- and meta-data management concepts; 
Analyse data architecture for consistency, completeness, accuracy and reasonableness; 
Contributing for the analysis of data management vision, strategy and policy and derive the IT requirements; 
Contributing to the design of the IT architecture considering master- and meta-data management concepts. 
Perform risk management and act proactively against project threats. 
Manage Issues, Changes and Decisions and document them thoroughly in the project logs. 
Participate to the preparation of Business cases, Vision documents, Project Charters. 
Propose solutions by combining IT tools and business process refactoring or improvements based on an in-depth understanding of a specific business domain. 
Working with EU legislation and extracting requirements from legal documents. 
Support in transitioning from Project to Operations
What do you need to succeed in this position?
Master + 11 years of relevant experience
At least 3 years of experience in:
Evaluating, comparing, selecting, and optimizing AI/ML models and architectures based on performance, operational constraints, and risk considerations. 
Applying machine-learning techniques including supervised, unsupervised, semi-supervised, and reinforcement learning (e.g. k-NN, Naive Bayes, SVM, decision forests, neural networks, and AI frameworks). 
Hands-on model evaluation, hyperparameter tuning, task-specific optimization, and resolution of model output issues. 
Developing and integrating model-serving endpoints and LLM APIs. 
Professional software development using Python, Unix/Linux, Bash, and modern development practices. 
Working with cloud platforms (AWS or Azure) and AI-related services, including ML pipelines, managed training, inference, and data storage. 
Using SQL, distributed query engines, big-data SQL, and information extraction techniques (recent experience).
At least 2 years of experience in:
Data management with large datasets, including data collection, selection, preprocessing, cleaning, and organization using coding and no-code tools. 
Working with NoSQL databases such as MongoDB, Cassandra, or HBase.
At least 1 year of experience in:
Working with unstructured data, non-relational databases, and data lakes. 
Operating in Agile development environments (experience from 2021 onward).
Ability to understand, speak and write in English C1; French at level B1 or higher will be an advantage; 
Desirable:
Experience in:
Quality assurance and quality control for machine translation (MT) and experience with MT quality procedures, testing methodologies and tools, such as automatic quality metrics (BLEU scores and similar) and human evaluation of MT quality. 
Deep learning covering common neural networks like perceptrons, feed-forward, backpropagation, and convolutional and recurrent neural networks. 
Perl, Matlab and R. 
Business-to-government definitions and related IT systems. 
DevOps practices. 
The following certifications are an asset : 
AWS Certified Machine Learning 
Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate 
SAS Certified Professional AI and Machine Learning Certification. 
PM2-Agile certification 
At Uni Systems, we are providing equal employment opportunities and banning any form of discrimination on grounds of gender, religion, race, color, nationality, disability, social class, political beliefs, age, marital status, sexual orientation or any other characteristics. Take a look at our Diversity, Equality & Inclusion Policy for more information.

Responsibilities also cover defining data rules, ensuring data integrity, designing IT architecture for NLP/ML/AI solutions, and performing risk management.

A minimum of 2 years of experience in large dataset management and 1 year in handling unstructured data within Agile environments is also required.

title: Senior Specialist in Stroke Medicine - Government Facility
locations_alt_raw: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
locations_derived: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is looking for a Senior Specialist in Stroke Medicine to join our team within a government facility. In this leadership role, you will be responsible for overseeing the clinical management of stroke patients, ensuring adherence to best practices and protocols in stroke care. You will work closely with a team of healthcare professionals to enhance patient outcomes while also contributing to community education and advocacy for stroke prevention and care.
Key Responsibilities
Lead the assessment, diagnosis, and management of patients with stroke and related neurovascular disorders.
Develop and implement comprehensive treatment plans tailored to individual patient needs.
Provide mentorship and training to junior staff and medical trainees.
Collaborate with other specialists to create integrated care pathways.
Engage in clinical research initiatives to improve stroke care protocols.
Participate in public health campaigns to raise awareness about stroke prevention.
Medical degree (M.D. or D.O.) from an accredited institution.
Board certification in Neurology or a relevant specialty with a focus on stroke medicine.
At least 7 years of clinical experience in stroke care.
Proven leadership skills and the ability to mentor clinical staff.
Strong understanding of stroke assessment and management protocols.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Commitment to community engagement and improving public health outcomes.
Good salary packages plus other standard benefits like accommodation, annual air tickets, family benefits, medical insurance, etc.

title: Chargé(e) d'Affaires Commercial
description_text:
SOMMAIRE DU POSTE
S’assurer que les relations avec les clients sont développées et maintenues en fournissant d’excellents produits et services financiers qui ajoutent de la valeur au client tout en minimisant les coûts et les risques pour la banque.
TÂCHES ET RESPONSABILITÉS SPÉCIFIQUES
1. Régulier/Routine
1. Développement de nouvelles affaires
2. Traitement des crédits et évaluations
3. Surveillance étroite des relations de crédit et des recouvrements de prêts
4. Entretenir de bonnes relations publiques avec les clients actuels et potentiels 
5. Atteindre les objectifs fixés en matière d’ECP et de bilan
6. Analyser et interpréter les données financières et faire des recommandations sur la solvabilité du client
7. Assister aux réunions du comité de crédit avec le Responsable d’équipe
8. Préparer des rapports périodiques pour l’équipe
9. Aider à la préparation du budget de l’équipe
10. Atteindre les objectifs de PCE, d'équilibre et d'autres objectifs de performance 
2. Tâches spéciales et autres
S'acquitter de toutes autres tâches qui peuvent lui être confiées par son Supérieur Hierarchique.
SPÉCIFICATIONS
Formation initiale : Minimum BAC+3 dans n'importe quelle discipline. Le diplôme de master est un atout.
Formation Professionnelle : Une certification professionnelle reconnue sera un 
avantage supplémentaire.
Expérience minimale : 3 ans d'expériences pertinente dans le métier commercial dans le secteur bancaire 
Connaissances, compétences et aptitudes requises : 
1. Bon service à la clientèle / gestion de la relation
2. Solides compétences en matière de vente et de négociation
3. Solides capacités d’analyse et d’interprétation financières
4. Bonne communication orale et écrite
5. Forte orientation stratégique
Compétences génériques
· Travail d’équipe
· Initiative
· Entreprenariat / prise en charge
· Capacité d’analyse / Résolution de problèmes
· Autogestion

title: Junior Executive - Finance & Administration
description_text:
Inivos (www.inivosglobal.com) is globally recognized for its expertise in digital transformation, focusing on Enterprise Solutions, Product Development, and Staff Augmentation. Committed to innovation, our team of consultants, developers, and quality engineers leads the way in setting new industry benchmarks. Within just five years, we've established a strong client base across 8+ continents, highlighting our leadership in groundbreaking solutions. 
Discover dynamic opportunities in Product Development and collaborate with our global team through Staff Augmentation and play a crucial role in advancing technology with Inivos Global. 
Job Description 
Finance Responsibilities 
Handle end-to-end payment processing, including vendor and employee payments. 
Maintain daily bookkeeping entries in accounting software. 
Verify invoices and supporting documents before processing payments. 
Prepare and maintain necessary schedules/reconciliation.
Manage and reconcile petty cash, ensuring proper documentation.
Prepare tax computations for direct and indirect taxes under guidance. 
Support timely tax filings and statutory compliance.
Maintain proper filing of financial and tax-related documents.
Support finance team during audits and month-end activities.
Administrative Responsibilities 
Coordinate with internal departments for purchase requirements and approvals.
Maintain vendor records and contracts, ensuring timely renewals.
Handling vendor management and follow-ups for payments and services.
Handle employee OPD / medical claims, verification, and record-keeping.
Maintain stationery stock records and coordinate procurement.
Ensure proper documentation and filing of administrative records.
Support general office administration and day-to-day operational tasks (Parking arrangements / Travelling Arrangements etc).
Pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration or a equally relevant qualification.
Minimum 1 to 2 years’ experience in the field of finance & administration. 
Basic knowledge of accounting principles. 
Proficiency in MS Excel and accounting software (QB / Zoho).
Good attention to detail and organizational skills.
Ability to handle multiple tasks and meet deadlines. 
Basic communication and coordination skills.
Trustworthy, responsible, and willing to learn.
Competitive compensation. 
Recognition & Appreciation.
Training & certifications funded. 
Open work Culture. 
Recreational Activities and Events. 
Medical claims & Insurance upon eligibility.
ai_core_responsibilities: Handle end-to-end payment processing and maintain daily bookkeeping entries. Support the finance team during audits and month-end activities while managing administrative tasks.
ai_requirements_summary: A Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration is required, along with 1 to 2 years of experience in finance and administration. Candidates should have basic knowledge of accounting principles and proficiency in MS Excel and accounting software.

title: Senior Consultant Neurosurgeon - Private Facility
locations_alt_raw: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
locations_derived: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
description_text:
SearchPlus HR is excited to announce an opportunity for a Senior Consultant Neurosurgeon to join a leading private medical facility known for its commitment to excellence in patient care. In this critical role, you will leverage your extensive expertise in neurosurgery to diagnose and treat a wide range of neurological conditions.
As a Senior Consultant Neurosurgeon, you will be at the forefront of surgical innovation, responsible for performing complex neurosurgical procedures while managing a dedicated team of healthcare professionals. Your contributions will be pivotal in enhancing the quality of patient outcomes and advancing the surgical protocols within the facility.
Key Responsibilities:
Evaluate patients through detailed assessments, including medical history reviews and diagnostic imaging analysis, to establish appropriate treatment plans.
Perform a variety of intricate neurosurgical procedures with a focus on safety and effectiveness.
Lead and mentor a team of surgeons, residents, and medical staff, fostering an environment of continuous learning and collaboration.
Oversee pre-operative and post-operative care, ensuring patient safety and addressing complications as they arise.
Contribute to clinical research and stay updated on the latest advancements in neurosurgery and best practices.
Comply with all regulatory and ethical standards to uphold the integrity of healthcare delivery.
Medical degree and valid licensure to practice neurosurgery.
Completed residency in Neurosurgery, with board certification preferred.
Extensive experience in neurosurgery, particularly in a leadership role.
Demonstrated ability in performing advanced surgical techniques and handling complex cases.
Strong understanding of neuroanatomy and a commitment to using evidence-based practices.
Leadership qualities with the ability to mentor and inspire a multidisciplinary team.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills for effective patient and team interactions.
Capability to function under pressure, making critical decisions in high-stress environments.
Dedication to ongoing personal and professional development in the field of neurosurgery.
Willingness to adopt new technologies and surgical techniques as they evolve.
Private Health Insurance
Training & Development
Performance Bonus

title: Freelance Automotive Engineer with Python Experience - AI Trainer
locations_alt_raw: Belo Horizonte, State of Minas Gerais, Brazil
description_text:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency. 
Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.
What this opportunity involves 
While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may: 
Design graduate- and industry-level automotive engineering problems grounded in real practice; 
Evaluate AI-generated solutions for correctness, assumptions, and engineering logic; 
Validate analytical or numerical results using Python (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas); 
Improve AI reasoning to align with first principles and accepted engineering standards; 
Apply structured scoring criteria to assess multi-step problem solving. 
What we look for 
This opportunity is a good fit for automotive engineers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have: 
Degree in Automotive Engineering or related fields, e.g. Mechatronics, Manufacturing Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, etc. 
3+ years of professional automotive engineering experience 
Strong written English (C1/C2) 
Strong Python proficiency for numerical validation 
Stable internet connection 
Professional certifications (e.g., PE, CEng, PMP) and experience in international or applied projects are an advantage.
How it works 
Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid
Project time expectations 
For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10–20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active. 
Payment 
Paid contributions, with rates up to $13/hour* 
Fixed project rate or individual rates, depending on the project 
Some projects include incentive payments 
*Note: Rates vary based on expertise, skills assessment, location, project needs, and other factors. Higher rates may be offered to highly specialized experts. Lower rates may apply during onboarding or non-core project phases. Payment details are shared per project.

title: Data Engineer
description_text:
About us
uMed is a healthtech and data platform advancing clinical research through high-quality real-world and patient-generated data. We build secure, scalable data platforms that transform complex patient, survey, and clinical data into actionable insights for researchers, clinicians, and partners.
Who we are looking for
As a Data Engineer, you will support the development and operation of uMed’s data pipelines and analytics-ready datasets. You will work closely with the Senior Data Engineer, who will provide technical guidance and mentorship while offering opportunities to grow your skills in data ingestion, transformation, and analytics enablement.
This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building reliable data pipelines and wants to deepen their experience working on a modern, AWS-based data platform.
Data Engineering & Pipelines
Build and maintain data pipelines for structured and semi-structured data (e.g., surveys, EHR extracts, events, and operational systems).
Support ETL/ELT workflows from source ingestion through to analytics-ready outputs.
Troubleshoot pipeline failures and data issues, escalating and collaborating where needed.
Data Modelling & Analytics Support
Implement data transformations and models based on designs and guidance from the Senior Data Engineer and Product Architect.
Help maintain analytics-ready datasets in the data warehouse to support reporting and insights.
Contribute to datasets that support cross-study and cross-region (UK and US) analysis.
Data Quality & Reliability
Assist in implementing data quality checks and validation rules.
Help investigate data discrepancies and ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Contribute to documentation and data lineage as part of standard delivery.
Collaboration & Learning
Work closely with the Senior Data Engineer through code reviews, pairing, and technical feedback.
Collaborate with product and analytics teams to understand data requirements.
Follow and apply established best practices in data engineering, testing, and documentation.
Required Qualifications
Core Requirements
3–4 years of experience in data engineering or closely related roles.
Solid SQL skills and experience working with analytics-focused datasets.
Experience building or maintaining data pipelines in a cloud environment (AWS preferred).
Familiarity with semi-structured data (e.g., JSON, event data).
Technical Stack (Experience With Some of the Following)
AWS: S3, Redshift, RDS, Athena, DocumentDB (or equivalent).
Data transformation tools (e.g., dbt or similar).
Orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Step Functions, or similar).
Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
BI tools and analytics enablement (e.g., Zoho Analytics, Tableau, Power BI, or similar).
Nice to Have
Experience in healthcare, clinical research, or regulated data environments.
Exposure to data quality frameworks or monitoring.
Experience working with multi-region data platforms (UK and US).
Interest in analytics, insights, or AI-enabled data products.
Why Join Us?
Work on real-world healthcare data to make a meaningful impact.
Gain experience across the full data lifecycle, from ingestion to analytics.
Join a collaborative team building a growing data platform.
25 days’ holiday, plus your birthday off 🎉 and bank holidays on top 
Private healthcare through Vitality 
2 paid volunteering days per year 
A monthly allowance to spend via our flexible benefits portal 
One day a week working from a coworking space of your choice, if you want to mix up working from home 
Equipment setup allowance to make your home office work for you 
Enhanced maternity, paternity, and parental leave
ai_core_responsibilities: The Data Engineer will be responsible for building and maintaining data pipelines for structured and semi-structured data, supporting ETL/ELT workflows, and troubleshooting pipeline failures. They will also implement data transformations and models based on guidance to support reporting and insights.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 3-4 years of experience in data engineering, solid SQL skills, and experience building data pipelines in a cloud environment, preferably AWS. Familiarity with semi-structured data formats like JSON is also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Addressing Evidence Gaps in Life Science Research
linkedin_org_locations: 125 Cambridge Park Drive, Cambridge, MA 02140, US
linkedin_org_description:
uMed combines RWE with the power of patient generated data to address the evidence gaps in life science research. 

By leveraging uMed’s ACCESS Research Platform which is embedded across a global network of healthcare institutions, researchers can rapidly access and engage with patients to generate insights derived from the decentralised collection of electronic health records, clinical outcomes, patient-reported data and biosamples.

title: Android Software Qualification Engineer
description_text:
Android Software Qualification EngineerLocation: Bangalore, IndiaWorkplace Type: Onsite
About the RoleWe are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Android Software Qualification Engineer to join our dynamic team in Bangalore. In this role, you will be responsible for the software qualification of mobile SoCs on the Android platform. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure the quality and stability of our products. This role requires a deep understanding of the Android operating system, strong scripting skills, and excellent analytical abilities. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of mentoring peers and contributing to a collaborative work environment. You will be responsible for designing, developing, and executing test plans to validate the functionality and performance of various subsystems, including audio, camera, display, graphics, video, power, and NPU. Your expertise will be crucial in identifying and resolving software defects, ensuring that our products meet the highest standards of quality and reliability.
Key Responsibilities
Develop and execute comprehensive test plans for mobile SoC software qualification on Android.
Perform in-depth testing of various subsystems, including audio, camera, display, graphics, video, power, and NPU.
Identify, analyze, and report software defects, working closely with development teams to resolve issues.
Automate testing processes using scripting languages such as Python, Bash, or Perl.
Utilize Android CTS/VTS and custom test frameworks to ensure compliance with industry standards.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the quality and stability of our products.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of our testing methodologies and processes.
Mentor junior engineers and share knowledge within the team.
Maintain proficiency with Linux/Unix, Git, and CI tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, etc.).
Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback.

Required Skills & Qualifications
Bachelor’s (or higher) in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline.
≤ 10 years of hands-on experience in mobile SoC software qualification on Android (AOSP, HAL, kernel, device tree, init).
Deep knowledge of at least of the following subsystems: audio, camera, display, graphics, video, power, NPU, etc.
Strong scripting/automation skills (Python, Bash, Perl, or similar).
Familiarity with Android CTS/VTS or custom test frameworks.
Proficiency with Linux/Unix, Git, and CI tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, etc.).
Excellent analytical ability, clear written and spoken English, and a proven track record of mentoring peers without formal authority.
Experience with bug tracking and issue management systems (e.g., Jira).
Strong understanding of software development life cycle (SDLC) and testing methodologies.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Android Software Qualification Engineer will develop and execute comprehensive test plans for mobile SoC software qualification on the Android platform. They will identify and resolve software defects while collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure product quality and stability.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a Bachelor's degree in a relevant field and up to 10 years of hands-on experience in mobile SoC software qualification on Android. Strong scripting skills and familiarity with various subsystems are essential for this role.
