title: Προϊστάμενος/η Ορόφων (Season 2026)
organization: Santa Marina, a Luxury Collection resort, Mykonos
description_text:
Σε μια ιδιωτική πλαγιά στον Όρνο, με τη δική του παραλία και ατελείωτη θέα σε έναν ηλιόλουστο ορίζοντα, το Santa Marina, a Luxury Collection Resort, Mykonos είναι ένα εμβληματικό θέρετρο πολυτελείας 5* στο νησί της Μυκόνου. Τα 117 Δωμάτια, Σουίτες και Βίλες του είναι σχεδιασμένα με πολυτελές και κομψό στυλ, πλαισιώνονται από καταπράσινους κήπους και δύο εντυπωσιακές πισίνες. Σχεδιασμένο σαν ένα παραδοσιακό μυκονιάτικο χωριό που βρίσκεται σε μια πλαγιά που οδηγεί στη θάλασσα. Μόνο 10 λεπτά από το αεροδρόμιο, το λιμάνι και τη Χώρα της Μυκόνου, το Santa Marina, Mykonos βρίσκεται σε κοντινή απόσταση από όλα όσα έχει να προσφέρει το όμορφο νησί. 
Η γαστρονομία είναι μια εμπειρία, το Buddha-Bar Beach, το Elais Greek Mediterranean Restaurant, το Oasis Pool και το Beach Lounge, όλα ολοκληρώνουν αυτή την εμπειρία με ζεστή και εκλεπτυσμένη εξυπηρέτηση. Στο Gingko Spa του Santa Marina, η ευεξία και η χαλάρωση ευθυγραμμίζονται, προσφέροντάς την απόλυτη ηρεμία. Το Santa Marina είναι σαν ένα αποκλειστικό σημείο συνάντησης των καλοταξιδεμένων από όλο τον κόσμο.
Προϊστάμενος/η Ορόφων (Season 2026)
Στο Santa Marina, a Luxury Collection Resort, Mykonos, κάθε μέλος της ομάδας μας συμβάλλει με φροντίδα και επαγγελματισμό στη δημιουργία μιας αυθεντικής και αξέχαστης εμπειρίας φιλοξενίας για τους επισκέπτες μας.
Ως Προϊστάμενος/η Ορόφων, θα έχετε καθοριστικό ρόλο στην καθημερινή οργάνωση και επίβλεψη της ομάδας housekeeping, διασφαλίζοντας ότι τα δωμάτια των επισκεπτών και οι κοινόχρηστοι χώροι πληρούν τα υψηλότερα πρότυπα καθαριότητας, τάξης και παρουσίασης που χαρακτηρίζουν το Luxury Collection brand. 
Βασικές Αρμοδιότητες:
Οργάνωση, καθοδήγηση και επίβλεψη της ομάδας housekeeping στην καθημερινή λειτουργία, διασφαλίζοντας αποδοτικότητα και τήρηση υψηλών προτύπων
Εκπαίδευση και υποστήριξη του προσωπικού σε διαδικασίες, πρότυπα και βέλτιστες πρακτικές housekeeping, ενισχύοντας την επαγγελματική ανάπτυξη της ομάδας
Άμεση και επαγγελματική διαχείριση αιτημάτων ή παρατηρήσεων επισκεπτών, διασφαλίζοντας την άριστη εμπειρία φιλοξενίας
Διαχείριση και παρακολούθηση των αποθηκών καθαρισμού, εξασφαλίζοντας επάρκεια υλικών και ορθή χρήση των πόρων
Διενέργεια τακτικών και λεπτομερών επιθεωρήσεων δωματίων και κοινόχρηστων χώρων, εξασφαλίζοντας την τήρηση των προτύπων καθαριότητας, οργάνωσης και συντήρησης
Τήρηση και εφαρμογή πρωτοκόλλων υγιεινής, ασφάλειας και λειτουργίας, διασφαλίζοντας πλήρη συμμόρφωση με τα πρότυπα του ξενοδοχείου
Συνεργασία με τα λοιπά τμήματα για την ενημέρωση σχετικά με την κατάσταση των δωματίων, τις προτιμήσεις των επισκεπτών και τυχόν τεχνικά ζητήματα
Τουλάχιστον 3 χρόνια εμπειρίας σε αντίστοιχη θέση, ιδανικά σε 5* ξενοδοχειακό περιβάλλον
Απόφοιτος σχολής τουριστικών επαγγελμάτων ή συναφούς κατεύθυνσης
Άριστη γνώση Αγγλικών, η γνώση επιπλέον γλωσσών αποτελεί πλεονέκτημα
Καλή γνώση εφαρμογών MS Office
Ικανότητα καθοδήγησης, ενθάρρυνσης και ανάπτυξης ομάδων
Άριστη προσοχή στη λεπτομέρεια και δέσμευση σε υψηλά πρότυπα καθαριότητας και εξυπηρέτησης
Ικανότητα προτεραιοποίησης καθηκόντων και αποτελεσματικής διαχείρισης χρόνου σε δυναμικό και απαιτητικό περιβάλλον
Ικανότητα συνεργασίας με διαφορετικά τμήματα και υποστήριξης άλλων ομάδων όποτε απαιτείται
Θετική, φιλική και επαγγελματική στάση, με έντονο προσανατολισμό στην εξυπηρέτηση των επισκεπτών
Νόμιμη δυνατότητα εργασίας στην Ελλάδα
Ανταγωνιστικό πακέτο αποδοχών, καθώς και παροχή διαμονής, γεύματα προσωπικού, υπηρεσία μεταφοράς (shuttle bus), στολή και υπηρεσίες πλυντηρίου
Συνεχείς ευκαιρίες εκπαίδευσης και ανάπτυξης, με στόχο την ενίσχυση των δεξιοτήτων σας και την εξέλιξη της καριέρας σας στον χώρο της πολυτελούς φιλοξενίας
Δυνατότητες επαγγελματικής εξέλιξης μέσω συμμετοχής σε task-force αποστολές σε ξενοδοχεία Marriott παγκοσμίως
Αποκλειστικές εκπτώσεις και ειδικές τιμές σε ξενοδοχεία Marriott για εσάς και τους αγαπημένους σας 
Στο Santa Marina, a Luxury Collection Resort, Mykonos, η φιλοξενία ξεκινά από τους ανθρώπους μας. Λειτουργούμε περήφανα σε ένα περιβάλλον εργασίας που βασίζεται στον σεβασμό, τη συνεργασία και τις ίσες ευκαιρίες για όλους. Αγκαλιάζουμε τη διαφορετικότητα και πιστεύουμε ότι οι εμπειρίες και οι προσωπικές διαδρομές κάθε μέλους της ομάδας μας εμπλουτίζουν το σύνολο. Οι άνθρωποί μας αποτελούν την καρδιά του ξενοδοχείου και τη μεγαλύτερή μας δύναμη, και καλωσορίζουμε με χαρά επαγγελματίες που θέλουν να εξελιχθούν και να πορευτούν μαζί μας.

Nestled on a private peninsula in Ornos, with its own private sandy beach and endless views of a sun-drenched horizon, Santa Marina, a Luxury Collection Resort, Mykonos is a very special place.
The resort’s 117 Rooms, Suites and Villas, all designed in a luxurious and chic style, are punctuated by lush gardens and two show-stopping infinity pools. 
Dining is an experience, a culinary and social occasion that is at the heart of this; Buddha-Bar Beach, Elais Greek Mediterranean Restaurant, Oasis Pool and the Beach Lounge, all complete this experience with warm and refined services. 
At Santa Marina's Gingko Spa wellness is a state of mind, a state of being, an alignment of both.

title: Brand Manager
description_text:
About Soleil Collective
Backed by Gauge Capital, Soleil Collective is building a portfolio of leading high-growth brands specializing in sunless tan, suncare, and bodycare products. Soleil accelerates growth and improves profitability with sales, marketing, operations, and strategic management expertise. We have completed two acquisitions to date: Loving Tan and Coco & Eve.

Loving Tan is the category leader in prestige tanning, delivering the most natural-looking tan. Proudly made on Australia’s famed Gold Coast, the professional quality, natural olive color, and flawless application are beloved by A-listers, creators, and beauty lovers alike.
Coco & Eve’s story starts in Bali, among the island’s volcanic beaches and lush rainforests. The brand allows you to glow up like you just returned from a trip to paradise with our sun-kissed, skin-loving tan products and deliciously scented, efficacious haircare—all formulated with the best tropical Balinese ingredients.
About the Job
This is a hands-on, high-impact role for a true marketing generalist — someone equally comfortable moving between strategy and execution, creative excellence and commercial performance, brand storytelling and real-world retail impact. Reporting into the Global Brand Director, the Brand Manager will own the day-to-day execution of brand strategy, translating big ideas into best-in-class campaigns and content across global markets to strengthen brand equity and grow Coco & Eve’s position as a global leader in feel-good botanical beauty. 
The role is deeply collaborative, partnering closely with Growth, Social, Influencer, Creative, Innovation, E-commerce and Retail teams to ensure cohesive, on-brand execution at every consumer touchpoint.

Responsibilities
Brand Strategy & Execution 
Support the development and execution of annual and quarterly brand plans aligned to global business priorities. 
Lead the day-to-day roll-out of brand initiatives, ensuring strategic intent translates clearly into execution. 
Act as a brand guardian, maintaining consistency of voice, tone, visual identity and messaging across all channels 
Campaign & Go-To-Market Ownership 
Own end-to-end execution of key brand campaigns and GTM moments — from briefing through to launch and optimisation. 
Develop clear, inspiring briefs for creative, content, social, influencer and paid teams. 
Partner with Innovation teams to support launch readiness and storytelling 
Act as the Brand’s internal north star across architecture, narrative and touchpoints, ensuring all campaign activity is aligned to overarching brand positioning 
Content Strategy & Storytelling 
Own annual and quarterly content strategy aligned to brand narratives and GTM priorities across all owned channels. 
Build and maintain a focused editorial calendar spanning hero campaigns, social, eDMs, blog content, and evergreen storytelling — rooted in strategic intent, not output volume. 
Ensure content ladders up to clear brand stories and plays distinct, intentional roles across the funnel (awareness, consideration, conversion and retention). 
Oversee the delivery and quality of flagship brand assets, including hero campaigns, tentpole moments, weekly eDM series and blog pillars, ensuring consistency of voice, tone and message. 
Own weekly eDM briefing process and asset approval, working closely w. the CRM Specialist on metric content optimizations 
Partner closely with E-commerce and Growth teams to ensure eDM and blog content supports campaign performance, SEO priorities and lifecycle engagement. 

Cross-Functional Collaboration 
Drive 360 marketing plans, ensuring seamless execution globally w. consistent brand and product storytelling across major retail partners, notably Ulta and Sephora. 
Partner closely with Social & Influencer teams to ensure campaign narratives translate natively across platforms. 
Collaborate with Growth and E-commerce teams to ensure brand campaigns are designed to perform across paid, owned and lifecycle channels. 
Work with Retail and Sales teams to support brand alignment on key moments, toolkits and presentations 

Performance, Insights & Optimisation 
Track and analyse campaign and content performance across channels. 
Translate insights into clear learnings and recommendations to inform future planning. 
Define success metrics beyond vanity KPIs, with an understanding of how brand activity supports growth and conversion. 

Experience & Background 
5 to 8 years’ experience in brand, marketing or content roles, ideally within beauty, wellness or FMCG. 
Proven experience delivering integrated brand campaigns and go-to-market launches. 
Strong understanding of social, content and digital ecosystems, and how brand storytelling shows up across them. 

Skills & Attributes 
Strategic thinker with a strong bias toward execution. 
Excellent project management skills with the ability to juggle multiple workstreams. 
Confident collaborator who can influence cross-functionally without direct authority. 
Data-literate, with the ability to turn performance signals into action. 
High attention to detail with a strong eye for brand quality and consistency. 
Genuine passion for beauty, trends, culture and consumer behaviour. 
Roll-up-your sleeves mentality, and highly intentional with budget management for strong ROI outputs 
Why Join Us? 
Be part of a globally scaling beauty brand with strong creative ambition. 
Work closely with senior brand leadership and cross-functional experts. 
Own meaningful brand moments with visibility and impact. 
A culture that values creativity, pace, accountability and collaboration. 
Extra Superannuation - Pension plan
Work From Home - Flexible hours
Training & Development
Employee Discounts
Product Allowance
ai_core_responsibilities: The Brand Manager will own the day-to-day execution of brand strategy, translating strategic ideas into best-in-class campaigns and content across global markets to strengthen brand equity for Coco & Eve. This involves leading campaign execution, owning content strategy across owned channels, and ensuring cohesive, on-brand execution across all consumer touchpoints.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 5 to 8 years of experience in brand, marketing, or content roles, ideally within beauty, wellness, or FMCG, with proven experience delivering integrated brand campaigns and go-to-market launches. Essential attributes include being a strategic thinker with a strong bias toward execution, excellent project management skills, and data literacy to translate performance signals into action.
linkedin_org_description:
Soleil Collective is a premier group of high-growth beauty brands specializing in sunless tan, suncare and bodycare products, backed by Gauge Capital, a preeminent middle-market investment firm. We partner with passionate founders and innovative global brands to step-change growth and expand presence, bringing top talent and resources to propel them to the next level. 

With decades of combined industry knowledge and innovation expertise, Soleil Collective brands—including Loving Tan, Coco & Eve and more to come—​​offer consumers around the world premium products with the highest quality experiences. We continue to be on the lookout for brands to partner with and for new venture opportunities within beauty.

About Gauge Capital
Gauge Capital is a leading middle-market private equity firm based in Southlake, TX. Gauge invests in five key sectors: business services, food & consumer, government & industrial services, healthcare and technology. The firm has been named multiple times in recent years as one of the top private equity firms for founders by Inc. Magazine. Gauge has also been added to the Top 50 PE Firms in the Middle Market by Grady Campbell on several occasions, and recently ranked in the top 5 out of 517 private equity firms in the HEC Paris – DowJones Small-Cap Buyout Performance Ranking.

title: Founding GTM Engineer
description_text:
Founding GTM Engineer
Location: San Francisco, CA (In-person) 
Compensation: $100K – $140K • 0.01% – 0.10% Equity
We are building the premier software platform designed to help elite engineering teams move faster. Trusted by over 150+ high-growth customers and backed by a $4.2M seed round from top-tier investors (including Y Combinator), we have maintained double-digit month-over-month growth since mid-2025.
As our Founding GTM (Go-To-Market) Engineer, you will be the bridge between our technical product and our customers. This is a rare "ground floor" opportunity to work directly with our CEO (who scaled multiple teams from $0–$30M ARR) and our CTO (formerly a founding engineer at a successful startup). You aren't just selling; you are shaping how millions of engineers and leaders understand their work.
What you’ll do:
Build the Engine: Do whatever it takes across systems, sales, and marketing to build a robust pipeline.
Technical Enablement: Create the assets that close deals—demos, reference architectures, integration guides, and technical documentation.
Automate Growth: Partner with the founders and product team to automate as much of the sales and onboarding process as possible.
Define the Playbook: We are inventing our outbound, pricing, and expansion strategies as we go. You will be a primary architect of these processes.
Stay Agile: One day you’ll be hunting new logos; the next, you’ll be designing a new internal system to streamline our operations.
Software Engineering Background: You have written production code and possess a deep understanding of modern development workflows, CI/CD, and the SDLC.
Exceptional Communication: You can translate complex technical concepts for both a DevOps engineer and a C-suite executive.
Bias for Action: You are self-motivated, comfortable with ambiguity, and wired for speed.
Entrepreneurial Spirit: You are excited about building a company from scratch, not just executing a pre-set plan.
Eligibility: Must be a US Citizen or current Visa holder.
Nice-to-Haves:
Experience selling developer tools, AI/ML platforms, or infrastructure software.
Prior experience at an early-stage startup (Seed to Series C), particularly in a Solutions Engineering or founding role.
Background as a former founder or early technical hire.
Direct Mentorship: Work daily with founders who have a proven track record of scaling startups to tens of millions in ARR.
Founding Equity: Own a piece of the company you are helping to build.
High Impact: Your work will directly influence the product roadmap and the company’s trajectory.
Market Competitive Pay: A strong base salary and comprehensive benefits package.
Trial Period: We value mutual fit—our hiring process includes a 3-5 day paid work trial to ensure we’re the right team for you.
Due to the high volume of applications we anticipate, we regret that we are unable to provide individual feedback to all candidates. If you do not hear back from us within 4 weeks of your application, please assume that you have not been successful on this occasion. We genuinely appreciate your interest and wish you the best in your job search.
Commitment to Equality and Accessibility:
At MLabs, we are committed to offer equal opportunities to all candidates. We ensure no discrimination, accessible job adverts, and providing information in accessible formats. Our goal is to foster a diverse, inclusive workplace with equal opportunities for all. If you need any reasonable adjustments during any part of the hiring process or you would like to see the job-advert in an accessible format please let us know at the earliest opportunity by emailing human-resources@mlabs.city.
MLabs Ltd collects and processes the personal information you provide such as your contact details, work history, resume, and other relevant data for recruitment purposes only. This information is managed securely in accordance with MLabs Ltd’s Privacy Policy and Information Security Policy, and in compliance with applicable data protection laws. title: Stage d'appui au Bureau des Déplacements Internationaux H/F/X - Lyon
description_text:
Lieu : Lyon 
Date souhaitée de démarrage : 16/03/2026
Durée : 6 mois 
Indignés face à l’injustice que vivent les personnes handicapées et les populations vulnérables, nous aspirons à un monde solidaire et inclusif, où toutes les différences nous enrichissent et où chacun peut vivre debout.
Handicap International est une organisation de solidarité internationale indépendante et impartiale, qui intervient dans les situations de pauvreté et d’exclusion, de conflits et de catastrophes. Œuvrant aux côtés des personnes handicapées et des populations vulnérables, elle agit et témoigne, pour répondre à leurs besoins essentiels, pour améliorer leurs conditions de vie et promouvoir le respect de leur dignité et de leurs droits fondamentaux.
Chez Handicap International, nous croyons fermement en l'importance de l'inclusion et de la diversité au sein de notre structure. C'est pourquoi nous sommes engagés dans une politique handicap afin de favoriser l'accueil et l'intégration de personnes en situation de handicap. 
Merci d’indiquer si vous avez besoin d’un aménagement particulier, y compris pour participer aux 1ers entretiens. 
Pour plus d’information sur l’association : www.hi.org
OBJECTIFS DU STAGE : 
Au sein de la Direction des Ressources Humaines, et plus particulièrement dans l’équipe du Bureau des Déplacements internationaux (BDDi), vous apporterez votre appui à l’organisation et à la gestion administrative des départs et retours du Personnel International en mission dans les pays d’intervention d’Humanité et Inclusion, notamment pour nos réponses d’urgence.
Vous pourrez également être amené(e) à travailler sur des projets transverses pour l’équipe
Activité 1 : Appui à l’Organisation des départs et retours de mission du personnel international 
Lancement des démarches relatives à l’obtention des visas
Réservation des vols et des billets de train 
Organisation du séjour à Lyon : hébergement, transport, per diem
Organisation des briefings/débriefings des expatriés 
Suivi du parcours médical du personnel international 
Envoi des informations départ en lien avec le terrain 
Activité 2 : Appui administratif pour le personnel international en mission
Au moment de l’embauche : constitution des dossiers administratifs du personnel international en lien avec le service RH et la Gestion Sociale
Suivi administratif du personnel international en mission (suivi des renouvellements : ordres de mission, avenants au contrat, etc.)
Gestion des demandes de remboursement du personnel international.
Activité 3 : Appui en communication interne
Conception de présentation d’un nouvel outil de réservation des titres de transports
Rédaction d’articles à destination de l’intranet sur les actualités du service
ACCESSIBILITE DU LIEU DE TRAVAIL : 
Les locaux sont facilement accessibles en transports en commun (bus, métro). Un parking voitures et un parc à vélos sont également à disposition. Au sein du bâtiment, des rampes d'accès et ascenseurs garantissent une meilleure circulation. Tous les postes de travail sont situés en Open Space mais des box sont disponibles à chaque étage pour travailler dans le calme si nécessaire. L’espace de travail est très lumineux. 
Une référente handicap est présente pour répondre aux éventuelles questions et vous accompagner dans vos démarches. En fonction de vos besoins, le poste peut être aménagé.
Vous êtes diplômé(e) d’un DUT, Licence ou Master généraliste (droit, commerce, gestion administrative, RH, humanitaire…)
Vous êtes curieux(-se) et souhaitez acquérir une expérience dans le milieu de la solidarité internationale Vous faites preuve de rigueur, d’organisation et de polyvalence, ce qui vous permet de traiter de nombreuses informations en parallèle 
Vous aimez travailler dans un contexte interculturel et vous maîtrisez le français et l’anglais professionnel 
Diversité encouragée, candidat(e) en situation de handicap bienvenu(e).
Vous recevrez : Une gratification mensuelle de 693€ 
Ce que vous trouverez chez nous :
Des valeurs fortes : Humanité, Inclusion, Engagement et intégrité ! Et une proximité avec le terrain via des conférences régulières.
Une organisation flexible : carte tickets resto (prise en charge 60% par HI), charte de télétravail : 8 jours de présentiel par mois minimum 
Transport : Prise en charge 50% des abonnements transports. 
Bien-être et santé : sensibilisations internes diverses, un pôle Qualité de Vie et Conditions de Travail actif
CSE et avantages sociaux : Œuvres sociales du CSE pour améliorer votre quotidien.
Une vie associative culturelle, sportive et sociale dynamique
Vous devez disposer d’une convention de stage obligatoire délivrée par un établissement d’enseignement ou de formation français. 
POSTULER :
Les stages proposés chez HI donnent l’occasion aux étudiants d’évoluer dans une organisation internationale et de prendre part à l’action d’Humanité & Inclusion. Nous considérons que les stagiaires pour qui l’expérience s’est bien passée sont nos talents de demain ! 
Merci d’envoyer votre CV et Lettre de motivation à : t.kravos@hi.org
ai_core_responsibilities: The intern will support the organization and administrative management of international staff departures and returns, particularly for emergency responses, including visa processes, travel booking, and medical follow-up. Additionally, the role involves administrative support for international staff on mission, such as file creation and contract tracking, alongside internal communication tasks.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must hold a DUT, Bachelor's, or Master's degree in a general field like law, business, administrative management, HR, or humanitarian studies. Applicants should be curious, rigorous, organized, versatile, and proficient in professional French and English, with a willingness to work in an intercultural context.
linkedin_org_locations: 138 avenue des Frères Lumière, Lyon, 69008, FR
linkedin_org_description: Humanity & Inclusion is an international organisation specialised in the field of disability.  Non-governmental, non-religious, non-political and non-profit making, it works alongside people with disabilities, whatever the context, offering them assistance and supporting them in their efforts to become self-reliant. Since its creation, the organisation has set up programmes in approximately 60 countries and intervened in many emergency situations. It has a network of eight national associations (Belgium, Canada, France, Germany, Luxembourg, Switzerland, United Kingdom, USA) which provide human and financial resources, manage projects and raise awareness of Handicap International's actions and campaigns.

title: Junior Legal Adviser
description_text:
DFDL was established in 1994 and founded on a unique vision: to create an integrated legal and tax advisory ﬁrm, with in-depth knowledge of the developing jurisdictions in which we are based. Our dedicated professionals exhibit the acumen and insight necessary to assist you in navigating the legal and tax complexities and challenges. Drawing on a wide-ranging industry experience and finely tuned local knowledge in countries we operate in, we strive to provide concise, commercially focused and innovative advice, staying true to our original motto: “One Region. One Firm”.
DFDL has 12 offices including collaborating firms, in Bangladesh, Cambodia, Indonesia, Lao PDR, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand and Vietnam.
As a full-service firm, DFDL provides legal and tax services across a variety of industry sectors. To better serve our clients’ business needs, we have also organized our expertise into the following practice groups:
Anti-Trust and Competition 
Aviation and Logistics 
Banking and Finance 
Compliance and Investigations 
Corporate, Mergers and Acquisitions 
Dispute Resolution 
Employment
Energy, Natural Resources and Infrastructure 
Investment Funds 
Real Estate and Hospitality 
Restructuring 
Tax and Transfer Pricing 
Technology, Media and Telecoms 
Position
We are currently looking to recruit a qualified candidate to join our Bangkok office as a Junior Legal Adviser.
The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work in a multi-cultural environment with career development and personal growth opportunities.
Reports to: Managing Director, DFDL Thailand Partners 
Key external relationships: Clients
Key internal relationships: Senior Legal Advisers, Partners, Managing Director
Number of hiring: 01
Salary Range: Competitive commensurate with experience and qualifications
Position type: Full time: Monday to Friday - 9:00 a.m. to 18.00 p.m.
Location: Bangkok, Thailand 
Job Description
As a Junior Legal Adviser, you are expected to undertake the following activities and tasks:
Provide legal advice regarding M&A, corporate and commercial issues, including in respect of investments into Thailand. This may include due diligence, research, drafting and negotiating transactional agreements and liaising directly with clients. 
Conduct legal research on various subjects.
Coordinate with the government authorities and communicate with team and client on updating progress of works.
Provide general legal assistance to other lawyers in the office and other tasks reasonably associated with the foregoing.
Bachelor’s degree of laws (LL.B.) or a Master of laws (LL.M.)
1-4 years of experience with international law firm or multinational company
Proficient in both spoken and written Thai and English.
Strong practical knowledge of the Thai legal system 
Able to work under pressure and time constraint
Be enthusiastic person and effective team member
Position will be based in Bangkok and may require occasional travel up-country.
Applications should include a curriculum vitae or resume, accompanied by a covering letter outlining the candidate’s interest in and suitability for the position.
All applications and expressions of interest will be treated confidentially, being reviewed only by DFDL Senior Management and Human Resources team.
Please note that this position is open until filled. Applications will be reviewed and processed on a rolling basis and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Junior Legal Adviser, you will provide legal advice on M&A and corporate issues, conduct legal research, and coordinate with government authorities. You will also assist other lawyers in the office with various legal tasks.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree in law or a Master of laws, along with 1-4 years of experience in an international law firm or multinational company. Proficiency in Thai and English, strong knowledge of the Thai legal system, and the ability to work under pressure are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Leading international law firm specializing in developing markets and featured in the world's leading legal directories

linkedin_org_description:
DFDL was established in 1994 and founded on a unique vision: create an integrated legal and tax advisory ﬁrm, with in-depth knowledge of the developing Southeast Asia region. 

Our team has the knowledge and expertise to guide clients through the rules and challenges of rapidly changing legal and tax environments. 

We offer clear, commercially relevant, and innovative advice based on extensive business experience and expert local knowledge.

Our expertise and coverage include:
- 10 countries & 12 offices: Bangladesh, Cambodia*, Lao PDR, Indonesia*, Myanmar, Malaysia*, Philippines*, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam (*collaborating firms).
 - 4 desks: China, EU, India and the US.
- 13 Practices and Sectors: Anti-Trust and Competition, Aviation and Logistics, Banking and Finance, Compliance and Investigations, Corporate and M&A, Dispute Resolution, Employment, Energy Natural Resources and Infrastructure, Investment Funds, Real Estate and Hospitality, Restructuring, Technology, Media and Telecoms, and Tax and Transfer Pricing.

title: Radiologist / Radyolog / Assistant Radiologist - Veri Etiketleme
description_text:
Tiroid ultrason analizine odaklanan yapay zekâ destekli klinik görüntüleme projemizde görev almak üzere Tam Zamanlı ve/veya Yarı Zamanlı Radyolog veya Radyoloji Asistanı arıyoruz. Seçilen uzman, yeni nesil klinik karar destek sistemlerinin geliştirilmesine katkı sağlayacak yüksek kaliteli tıbbi veri etiketleme ve klinik doğrulama süreçlerinde yer alacaktır.
Etiketleme platformu ve iş akışı hakkında gerekli eğitimler sağlanacaktır.
Görev ve Sorumluluklar
Tiroid ultrason görüntü ve videolarını etiketlemek 
Nodül ve çevresini proje platformu üzerinden çizmek (segmentasyon) 
Nodülleri ACR TI-RADS kriterlerine göre etiketlemek 
AI araştırmacıları ve veri ekipleriyle asenkron çalışmak 
Veri kalitesi, hasta gizliliği ve klinik uygunluk standartlarını korumak 
Radyoloji uzmanı veya radyoloji asistanı olmak 
Haftada en az 3 gün zaman ayırabilmek 
Radyoloji raporlama süreçlerine hakim olmak 
Tiroid ultrason değerlendirmesinde deneyim sahibi olmak 
Uzaktan ve bağımsız çalışmaya uygun olmak 
Esnek ve uzaktan çalışma modeli 
Medikal yapay zekâ Ar-Ge projesinde aktif rol 
Akademik işbirliği ve yayın potansiyeli 
Klinik etki yaratacak yenilikçi projeye katkı fırsatı
ai_core_responsibilities: The selected expert will label thyroid ultrasound images and videos, drawing nodules and their surroundings on the project platform. They will also work asynchronously with AI researchers and data teams while maintaining data quality and clinical compliance standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be a radiologist or radiology assistant with experience in thyroid ultrasound evaluation. They should be able to dedicate at least three days a week and be suitable for remote and independent work.
linkedin_org_slogan: Traick is an AI-based web application for the diagnosis of thyroid nodules.
linkedin_org_description: Traick is an AI-based web application for the diagnosis of thyroid nodules.

title: Lead UX Writer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Lead UX Writer in United Kingdom.
This is a high-impact opportunity to shape the voice, clarity, and usability of a premium mobile financial experience used by a global audience. As Lead UX Writer, you will own the content strategy across the entire product, transforming complex financial and crypto concepts into intuitive, human-centered experiences. You will work closely with product, design, engineering, legal, and data teams to embed content into the core of the design process. Your work will directly influence trust, engagement, and conversion across critical user journeys. Operating in a fully remote, fast-paced environment, you will combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution. This role offers both creative ownership and strategic influence, making it ideal for someone passionate about clarity, accessibility, and impactful design.
\n

Accountabilities:
Define and lead the end-to-end content strategy, voice, and tone guidelines for the mobile application, ensuring clarity, consistency, and brand alignment.
Audit and optimize key user flows to reduce friction, enhance usability, and improve trust across financial and crypto interactions.
Craft microcopy, onboarding flows, instructional content, and error messaging that simplify complex transactions and empower users.
Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, engineers, legal, and compliance teams to embed content early in the design process.
Translate regulatory and compliance requirements into transparent, user-friendly language without sacrificing accuracy.
Conduct copy testing, user research, and funnel analysis to continuously improve conversion, comprehension, and retention.
Champion content design best practices across the organization, driving education, alignment, and continuous improvement.
Requirements:
5+ years of experience in UX writing or content design, ideally within fintech, wealth management, or cryptocurrency environments.
Proven ability to simplify complex financial concepts and transform dense workflows into intuitive user experiences.
Strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership of product flows, including onboarding, transactions, and troubleshooting.
Experience working within regulated environments, balancing legal constraints with user-centered clarity.
Deep understanding of accessibility standards, inclusive design principles, and mobile-first design patterns.
Familiarity with behavioral economics and how language influences trust and decision-making.
Proficiency with modern design tools such as Figma or Sketch and experience working directly within design files.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to justify decisions using data, research, and structured reasoning.
Self-starter mindset, comfort in fast-moving environments, and experience working within agile, cross-functional teams.
Benefits:
Fully remote work environment with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Flexible paid time off to support work-life balance.
Annual professional development budget for learning, courses, and conferences.
Competitive compensation package.
Collaborative, high-performance culture with global teammates.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge financial and crypto technology products.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.


#LI-CL1

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of experience in UX writing or content design, preferably in fintech or cryptocurrency. A strong portfolio demonstrating ownership of product flows and familiarity with regulatory environments is essential.

Jobgether is an AI-powered career coach and matching platform fixing the broken job search. Remote professionals no longer waste hours applying blindly; instead, they receive a personalized job search strategy, stronger visibility, and curated matches aligned with their skills, flexibility preferences, and career goals.

We flip the hiring model by connecting talent only to roles that truly match, reducing noise for employers and eliminating wasted effort for candidates. Our purpose is to make remote job search guided and intentional.

title: Head of Sales (Remote from Turkey)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Sales in Turkey.
This senior leadership role offers a unique opportunity to design, build, and scale a high-impact B2B sales organization within a fast-growing, tech-enabled hospitality environment. You will own the full sales strategy, driving revenue growth across corporate, group, and extended-stay segments while building a best-in-class team and operating model. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, you will align sales execution with broader commercial, marketing, and operational goals. This role combines strategic vision with hands-on deal-making, offering broad influence over market expansion and brand positioning. You will lead from the front, securing key national and enterprise accounts while embedding scalable systems and processes. If you thrive in dynamic, high-growth environments and enjoy building teams and revenue engines from the ground up, this role offers exceptional scope and impact.
\n

Accountabilities:
Architect, build, and lead a high-performing sales organization, including hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management.
Define and execute the corporate and group sales strategy to deliver ambitious revenue and growth targets.
Personally source, negotiate, and close high-impact national and enterprise accounts across priority segments.
Establish scalable sales processes, forecasting models, reporting cadences, and accountability frameworks.
Drive disciplined pipeline management, accurate forecasting, and data-driven performance reviews.
Collaborate closely with revenue management, marketing, operations, and distribution to ensure end-to-end commercial alignment.
Represent the organization at industry events, conferences, and strategic partner engagements.
Requirements:
10+ years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership.
Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading high-performing sales teams.
Demonstrated success securing and growing national or enterprise-level accounts.
Strong operational rigor, forecasting expertise, and comfort with performance analytics and KPIs.
Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Experience thriving in high-growth, fast-paced, tech-enabled environments.
Bonus points for experience across traditional hotels and alternative accommodations, extended-stay programs, and multi-market enterprise sales.
Benefits:
Highly competitive total compensation package with base salary and performance-based incentives.
Equity participation and long-term incentive plans.
Flexible, remote-first working environment.
Generous paid time off policy.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Retirement savings plans and employer contributions.
Travel credits, accommodation discounts, and exclusive employee perks.
Home office, connectivity, and wellness stipends.

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. title: Head of Sales (Remote from Switzerland)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Sales in Switzerland.
This senior leadership role offers a unique opportunity to design, build, and scale a high-impact B2B sales organization within a fast-growing, tech-enabled hospitality environment. You will own the full sales strategy, driving revenue growth across corporate, group, and extended-stay segments while building a best-in-class team and operating model. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, you will align sales execution with broader commercial, marketing, and operational goals. This role combines strategic vision with hands-on deal-making, offering broad influence over market expansion and brand positioning. You will lead from the front, securing key national and enterprise accounts while embedding scalable systems and processes. If you thrive in dynamic, high-growth environments and enjoy building teams and revenue engines from the ground up, this role offers exceptional scope and impact.
\n

Accountabilities:
Architect, build, and lead a high-performing sales organization, including hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management.
Define and execute the corporate and group sales strategy to deliver ambitious revenue and growth targets.
Personally source, negotiate, and close high-impact national and enterprise accounts across priority segments.
Establish scalable sales processes, forecasting models, reporting cadences, and accountability frameworks.
Drive disciplined pipeline management, accurate forecasting, and data-driven performance reviews.
Collaborate closely with revenue management, marketing, operations, and distribution to ensure end-to-end commercial alignment.
Represent the organization at industry events, conferences, and strategic partner engagements.
Requirements:
10+ years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership.
Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading high-performing sales teams.
Demonstrated success securing and growing national or enterprise-level accounts.
Strong operational rigor, forecasting expertise, and comfort with performance analytics and KPIs.
Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Experience thriving in high-growth, fast-paced, tech-enabled environments.
Bonus points for experience across traditional hotels and alternative accommodations, extended-stay programs, and multi-market enterprise sales.
Benefits:
Highly competitive total compensation package with base salary and performance-based incentives.
Equity participation and long-term incentive plans.
Flexible, remote-first working environment.
Generous paid time off policy.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Retirement savings plans and employer contributions.
Travel credits, accommodation discounts, and exclusive employee perks.
Home office, connectivity, and wellness stipends.

\n

title: Head of Sales (Remote from Mexico)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Sales in Mexico.
This senior leadership role offers a unique opportunity to design, build, and scale a high-impact B2B sales organization within a fast-growing, tech-enabled hospitality environment. You will own the full sales strategy, driving revenue growth across corporate, group, and extended-stay segments while building a best-in-class team and operating model. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, you will align sales execution with broader commercial, marketing, and operational goals. This role combines strategic vision with hands-on deal-making, offering broad influence over market expansion and brand positioning. You will lead from the front, securing key national and enterprise accounts while embedding scalable systems and processes. If you thrive in dynamic, high-growth environments and enjoy building teams and revenue engines from the ground up, this role offers exceptional scope and impact.
\n

Accountabilities:
Architect, build, and lead a high-performing sales organization, including hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management.
Define and execute the corporate and group sales strategy to deliver ambitious revenue and growth targets.
Personally source, negotiate, and close high-impact national and enterprise accounts across priority segments.
Establish scalable sales processes, forecasting models, reporting cadences, and accountability frameworks.
Drive disciplined pipeline management, accurate forecasting, and data-driven performance reviews.
Collaborate closely with revenue management, marketing, operations, and distribution to ensure end-to-end commercial alignment.
Represent the organization at industry events, conferences, and strategic partner engagements.
Requirements:
10+ years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership.
Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading high-performing sales teams.
Demonstrated success securing and growing national or enterprise-level accounts.
Strong operational rigor, forecasting expertise, and comfort with performance analytics and KPIs.
Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Experience thriving in high-growth, fast-paced, tech-enabled environments.
Bonus points for experience across traditional hotels and alternative accommodations, extended-stay programs, and multi-market enterprise sales.
Benefits:
Highly competitive total compensation package with base salary and performance-based incentives.
Equity participation and long-term incentive plans.
Flexible, remote-first working environment.
Generous paid time off policy.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Retirement savings plans and employer contributions.
Travel credits, accommodation discounts, and exclusive employee perks.
Home office, connectivity, and wellness stipends.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Product Manager (Remote from India)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in India.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.
Paid parental leave.
Annual professional development allowance.
Long-term sabbatical program.
Comprehensive healthcare coverage, including dental and vision, depending on location.
Retirement and pension contribution plans based on region.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

They will partner closely with engineering and design to support development, testing, and release execution, and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least two years of experience in a Product Manager or similar role within a SaaS or technology environment, with proven ability to shape and maintain a product roadmap. title: Product Manager (Remote from Philippines)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in Philippines.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.
Paid parental leave.
Annual professional development allowance.
Long-term sabbatical program.
Comprehensive healthcare coverage, including dental and vision, depending on location.
Retirement and pension contribution plans based on region.

\n

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Sales in Czech Republic.
This senior leadership role offers a unique opportunity to design, build, and scale a high-impact B2B sales organization within a fast-growing, tech-enabled hospitality environment. You will own the full sales strategy, driving revenue growth across corporate, group, and extended-stay segments while building a best-in-class team and operating model. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, you will align sales execution with broader commercial, marketing, and operational goals. This role combines strategic vision with hands-on deal-making, offering broad influence over market expansion and brand positioning. You will lead from the front, securing key national and enterprise accounts while embedding scalable systems and processes. If you thrive in dynamic, high-growth environments and enjoy building teams and revenue engines from the ground up, this role offers exceptional scope and impact.
\n

Accountabilities:
Architect, build, and lead a high-performing sales organization, including hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management.
Define and execute the corporate and group sales strategy to deliver ambitious revenue and growth targets.
Personally source, negotiate, and close high-impact national and enterprise accounts across priority segments.
Establish scalable sales processes, forecasting models, reporting cadences, and accountability frameworks.
Drive disciplined pipeline management, accurate forecasting, and data-driven performance reviews.
Collaborate closely with revenue management, marketing, operations, and distribution to ensure end-to-end commercial alignment.
Represent the organization at industry events, conferences, and strategic partner engagements.
Requirements:
10+ years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership.
Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading high-performing sales teams.
Demonstrated success securing and growing national or enterprise-level accounts.
Strong operational rigor, forecasting expertise, and comfort with performance analytics and KPIs.
Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Experience thriving in high-growth, fast-paced, tech-enabled environments.
Bonus points for experience across traditional hotels and alternative accommodations, extended-stay programs, and multi-market enterprise sales.
Benefits:
Highly competitive total compensation package with base salary and performance-based incentives.
Equity participation and long-term incentive plans.
Flexible, remote-first working environment.
Generous paid time off policy.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Retirement savings plans and employer contributions.
Travel credits, accommodation discounts, and exclusive employee perks.
Home office, connectivity, and wellness stipends.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves architecting, building, and leading a high-performing sales organization while defining and executing the corporate and group sales strategy to meet ambitious revenue targets. Responsibilities also include personally securing and closing high-impact national and enterprise accounts and establishing scalable sales processes and accountability frameworks.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess over 10 years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and proven success in building and scaling sales teams. linkedin_org_description:
Jobgether is an AI-powered career coach and matching platform fixing the broken job search. Remote professionals no longer waste hours applying blindly; instead, they receive a personalized job search strategy, stronger visibility, and curated matches aligned with their skills, flexibility preferences, and career goals.

We flip the hiring model by connecting talent only to roles that truly match, reducing noise for employers and eliminating wasted effort for candidates. Jobgether combines AI coaching, profile optimization, Match Score insights, and the world’s largest remote job database to help people land opportunities faster and with less bias.

Our purpose is to make remote job search guided and intentional. 
Our mission is to become the world’s reference platform for remote talent, ensuring no professional remains invisible and every match is meaningful.

title: Product Manager (Remote from Sweden)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in Sweden.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.
Paid parental leave.
Annual professional development allowance.
Long-term sabbatical program.
Comprehensive healthcare coverage, including dental and vision, depending on location.
Retirement and pension contribution plans based on region.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

ai_core_responsibilities: The Product Manager will own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities while driving prioritization decisions. They will partner closely with engineering and design to support development, testing, and release execution, and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.

description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in United Arab Emirates.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.
Paid parental leave.
Annual professional development allowance.
Long-term sabbatical program.
Comprehensive healthcare coverage, including dental and vision, depending on location.
Retirement and pension contribution plans based on region.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Head of Sales (Remote from Austria)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Head of Sales in Austria.
This senior leadership role offers a unique opportunity to design, build, and scale a high-impact B2B sales organization within a fast-growing, tech-enabled hospitality environment. You will own the full sales strategy, driving revenue growth across corporate, group, and extended-stay segments while building a best-in-class team and operating model. Partnering closely with cross-functional leaders, you will align sales execution with broader commercial, marketing, and operational goals. This role combines strategic vision with hands-on deal-making, offering broad influence over market expansion and brand positioning. You will lead from the front, securing key national and enterprise accounts while embedding scalable systems and processes. If you thrive in dynamic, high-growth environments and enjoy building teams and revenue engines from the ground up, this role offers exceptional scope and impact.
\n

Accountabilities:
Architect, build, and lead a high-performing sales organization, including hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management.
Define and execute the corporate and group sales strategy to deliver ambitious revenue and growth targets.
Personally source, negotiate, and close high-impact national and enterprise accounts across priority segments.
Establish scalable sales processes, forecasting models, reporting cadences, and accountability frameworks.
Drive disciplined pipeline management, accurate forecasting, and data-driven performance reviews.
Collaborate closely with revenue management, marketing, operations, and distribution to ensure end-to-end commercial alignment.
Represent the organization at industry events, conferences, and strategic partner engagements.
Requirements:
10+ years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, with deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership.
Proven track record of building, scaling, and leading high-performing sales teams.
Demonstrated success securing and growing national or enterprise-level accounts.
Strong operational rigor, forecasting expertise, and comfort with performance analytics and KPIs.
Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.
Experience thriving in high-growth, fast-paced, tech-enabled environments.
Bonus points for experience across traditional hotels and alternative accommodations, extended-stay programs, and multi-market enterprise sales.
Benefits:
Highly competitive total compensation package with base salary and performance-based incentives.
Equity participation and long-term incentive plans.
Flexible, remote-first working environment.
Generous paid time off policy.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance coverage.
Retirement savings plans and employer contributions.
Travel credits, accommodation discounts, and exclusive employee perks.
Home office, connectivity, and wellness stipends.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

This includes personally sourcing, negotiating, and closing high-impact national and enterprise accounts while establishing scalable sales processes and accountability frameworks.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess over 10 years of experience in hospitality or travel-industry B2B sales, demonstrating deep expertise in corporate account management and leadership. title: Product Manager (Remote from Canada)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in Canada.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.

title: Product Manager (Remote from Belgium)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager in Belgium.
This role offers the opportunity to lead the evolution of a high-impact SaaS product used by a global customer base, shaping features from concept to launch in a fast-moving, remote-first environment. You will define and own the product roadmap, translate business goals and user needs into clear priorities, and collaborate closely with engineering, design, and cross-functional teams. By balancing strategy, execution, and data-driven decision-making, you will drive meaningful improvements to product performance and customer satisfaction. This position is ideal for a proactive, organized, and analytical product leader who thrives in autonomy and continuous learning. You will play a central role in delivering reliable, scalable solutions that directly influence growth and user experience. If you enjoy turning complex problems into elegant, impactful products, this role provides both ownership and long-term career development.
\n

Accountabilities:
Own and maintain the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals, user needs, and technical priorities.
Drive prioritization decisions by balancing stakeholder input, engineering capacity, customer feedback, and strategic objectives.
Define product problems, scope, success criteria, and trade-offs to enable efficient and high-quality delivery.
Partner closely with engineering and product design to support development, testing, and release execution.
Communicate priorities, timelines, progress, and risks clearly to stakeholders across the organization.
Define, implement, track, and report product performance metrics to guide continuous improvement and strategic planning.
Requirements:
At least 2 years of experience in a Product Manager, Project Manager, or similar role within a SaaS or technology-driven environment.
Proven experience shaping, prioritizing, and maintaining a product roadmap.
Strong analytical skills and hands-on experience with product metrics and analytics tools.
Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly.
Highly organized with strong prioritization, time management, and problem-solving abilities.
Fast learner with the ability to understand new domains, complex systems, and evolving user needs.
Bonus points for experience with WordPress, Jira, Confluence, dashboard creation, and high-autonomy product ownership.
Benefits:
Competitive salary package.
Fully remote and flexible work environment.
Flexible paid time off.
Annual remote work expense budget.
Paid parental leave.
Annual professional development allowance.
Long-term sabbatical program.
Comprehensive healthcare coverage, including dental and vision, depending on location.
Retirement and pension contribution plans based on region.

\n
