title: Remote Senior Executive Area Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Executive Area Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will be responsible for driving sales and implementing effective strategies to enhance market penetration. You will work closely with various teams to ensure alignment with the company’s mission and objectives while providing exceptional support to clients. Your work will directly contribute to the growth and success of the organization, ultimately making a significant impact on healthcare innovations. The position allows for flexibility to operate from a remote setting, creating a balanced work-life experience.
\n

Accountabilities
Identify targets and execute selling strategies based on company direction through ethical selling practice.
Drive sales growth by implementing best practices to ensure proper targeting and reimbursement strategies.
Contribute key learnings to demonstrate a deep understanding of marketplace opportunities.
Utilize approved corporate literature and scientific data in communications with clients.
Provide reporting and tracking of sales activities along with territory forecasts.
Maintain a solid understanding of industry products and market trends.
Ensure compliance with company guidelines and relevant laws.
Assist with training and mentoring new team members.

Requirements
BS/BA in a related discipline or equivalent experience required.
5+ years of experience in the wound care field and medical sales is required.

Surgical experience and O.R./VAC Submission experience required.
Familiarity with skin substitutes is a plus.
Previous experience in startup and medium-sized companies advantageous.

title: Remote Power Platform Developer
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Power Apps Developer - REMOTE. This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation through the Microsoft Power Platform. You will lead the development and delivery of impactful solutions that enhance the agency's efficiency. The ideal candidate will work closely with stakeholders to ensure that their requirements are met effectively. With an emphasis on collaboration and innovation, this position not only focuses on technical development but also on improving user experiences across various applications. Join us to contribute to significant projects that make a difference in the public sector.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the design, development, and delivery of solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, analyze, and translate requirements into functional solutions and data visualization.
Provide solution architecture guidance and ensure scalability, security, and maintainability of applications.
Plan, create, configure, customize, and resolve issues related to SharePoint sites based on user requirements and security models.
Modify and update Power Apps forms as new information is released.
Document site updates and inform users on enhancements.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to Power Apps applications and integrations.
Implement and Configure Microsoft Dataverse and Connectors to support Power Platform solutions.
Stay current on Power Platform updates and emerging technologies to recommend innovative improvements.

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.
Eight (8) years’ experience in Microsoft technologies, including 3+ years of hands-on Power Platform development (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Strong understanding of solution architecture, data integration, connectors, APIs, and security models.
Experience with SQL, relational databases, and data integration.
Experience creating dashboards/Business Intelligence based on key performance indicators.
Experience providing custom solution client-side development and maintenance using Power Automate / Power Apps.
Experience providing user training, provisioning, and management of SharePoint solutions.
A team player with solid communication skills, excellent at problem solving, troubleshooting, and embracing new challenges.
Positive attitude and the ability to remain flexible in a dynamic environment.
This position requires a Secret clearance.

Benefits
Competitive salary based on experience, up to $160,000.
Flexible work hours and the ability to work remotely.
Opportunities for professional development and certification.
Collaborative environment with a focus on innovative solutions.
Engagement in impactful projects that benefit society.
Health and wellness benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.

title: Remote Finance Consultant
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.

title: Remote Financial Analyst
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Remote Lead Controller Assistant
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

title: Remote Financial Operations Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Remote Infrastructure Contract Attorney
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Contract Attorney - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in enhancing the legal framework for technology and infrastructure contracts. You will collaborate with various stakeholders, ensuring that contractual obligations are clearly defined and executed. This position requires a blend of legal expertise and collaborative skills to navigate the complexities of technology transactions. You'll provide essential support to ensure compliance with regulations and optimize legal processes. Your contributions will directly impact our partner’s operational efficiency and contractual success while working flexibly in a remote setting.
\n

Accountabilities
Draft, review, and negotiate a variety of technology and infrastructure contracts, including vendor agreements, hardware supply, and engineering support deals.
Provide transactional legal support across technology infrastructure initiatives.
Partner with in-house counsel and business stakeholders to deliver timely, business-focused solutions.
Review and redline contracts to align with corporate legal and business requirements.
Identify and escalate legal risks, ensuring compliance with client standards and playbooks.
Collaborate with leadership on workflow improvements and knowledge management.
Support quality control (QC) efforts, audits, and process documentation as needed.

Requirements
Prior in-house, managed services, or law firm experience with technology clients preferred.
Strong drafting, redlining, and negotiation skills.
Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently while collaborating with remote teams.
Comfortable working across time zones; availability to cover Pacific Time zone required.

Health, vision, and dental benefits.

Tuition discounts for continued education.
Flexible work schedule in a remote setting.
Collaborative team environment.

title: Remote Change Management Leader
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Change Management Consultant - REMOTE. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in guiding organizations through their digital transformation journey using Microsoft 365 technologies. Your expertise in Organizational Change Management (OCM) will directly impact the success of our clients and their ability to adopt new technologies effectively. By collaborating with cross-functional teams, you will develop and implement OCM strategies that address the unique needs of each client. Your work will not only enhance user adoption but also empower teams to utilize tools like Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive to their full potential. Join us to make a significant impact and drive meaningful change within organizations.
\n

Accountabilities
Work with the OCM Practice Manager and elite team on various projects.
Conduct OCM activities including vision discovery and change scope analysis.
Develop and implement new offerings related to Microsoft Copilot.
Strategize communication and training plans for digital transformation.
Educate stakeholders and users to ease the transition.
Measure and report the success of change initiatives.
Design change management plans using best practices.
Provide leadership and guidance to junior consultants.
Collaborate with project teams to align OCM efforts with project objectives.
Stay updated on emerging technologies to create new OCM materials.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in change management or adoption functions.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365 technologies including Copilot, Teams, and SharePoint.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
Experience with user research and post-implementation reviews.
Ability to proactively respond to internal/external inquiries.
Demonstrated expertise in OCM projects applied to Microsoft 365.
Experience with GCC High migration projects is a plus.
Preferred credentials include PROSCI Certification or Microsoft Adoption Specialist certification.
Proven ability to lead assessments with various levels of stakeholders.
Track record of delivering results and adapting to diverse cultures.

Benefits
Be part of a forward-thinking company that values innovation.
Work with a passionate team dedicated to making a difference.
Enjoy a flexible remote work environment.
Access to ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
Collaborate in a supportive and inclusive remote team.

title: Remote Controller Assistant
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options

title: Remote Change Management Strategist
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Change Management Consultant - REMOTE. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in guiding organizations through their digital transformation journey using Microsoft 365 technologies. Your expertise in Organizational Change Management (OCM) will directly impact the success of our clients and their ability to adopt new technologies effectively. By collaborating with cross-functional teams, you will develop and implement OCM strategies that address the unique needs of each client. Your work will not only enhance user adoption but also empower teams to utilize tools like Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive to their full potential. Join us to make a significant impact and drive meaningful change within organizations.
\n

Accountabilities
Work with the OCM Practice Manager and elite team on various projects.
Conduct OCM activities including vision discovery and change scope analysis.
Develop and implement new offerings related to Microsoft Copilot.
Strategize communication and training plans for digital transformation.
Educate stakeholders and users to ease the transition.
Measure and report the success of change initiatives.
Design change management plans using best practices.
Provide leadership and guidance to junior consultants.
Collaborate with project teams to align OCM efforts with project objectives.
Stay updated on emerging technologies to create new OCM materials.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in change management or adoption functions.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365 technologies including Copilot, Teams, and SharePoint.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
Experience with user research and post-implementation reviews.
Ability to proactively respond to internal/external inquiries.
Demonstrated expertise in OCM projects applied to Microsoft 365.
Experience with GCC High migration projects is a plus.
Preferred credentials include PROSCI Certification or Microsoft Adoption Specialist certification.
Proven ability to lead assessments with various levels of stakeholders.
Track record of delivering results and adapting to diverse cultures.

Benefits

Work with a passionate team dedicated to making a difference.
Enjoy a flexible remote work environment.
Access to ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

title: Remote Assistant Controller
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture
Health and wellness programs
Work-life balance support
Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

In this role, you will be responsible for driving sales and implementing effective strategies to enhance market penetration. You will work closely with various teams to ensure alignment with the company’s mission and objectives while providing exceptional support to clients. title: Remote Advertising Finance Manager
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Remote Change Management Advisor
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Change Management Consultant - REMOTE. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in guiding organizations through their digital transformation journey using Microsoft 365 technologies. Measure and report the success of change initiatives.

Provide leadership and guidance to junior consultants.

Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.

title: Power Platform Developer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Power Apps Developer - REMOTE. This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation through the Microsoft Power Platform. You will lead the development and delivery of impactful solutions that enhance the agency's efficiency. The ideal candidate will work closely with stakeholders to ensure that their requirements are met effectively. With an emphasis on collaboration and innovation, this position not only focuses on technical development but also on improving user experiences across various applications. Join us to contribute to significant projects that make a difference in the public sector.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the design, development, and delivery of solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, analyze, and translate requirements into functional solutions and data visualization.
Provide solution architecture guidance and ensure scalability, security, and maintainability of applications.
Plan, create, configure, customize, and resolve issues related to SharePoint sites based on user requirements and security models.
Modify and update Power Apps forms as new information is released.
Document site updates and inform users on enhancements.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to Power Apps applications and integrations.
Implement and Configure Microsoft Dataverse and Connectors to support Power Platform solutions.
Stay current on Power Platform updates and emerging technologies to recommend innovative improvements.

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.
Eight (8) years’ experience in Microsoft technologies, including 3+ years of hands-on Power Platform development (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Strong understanding of solution architecture, data integration, connectors, APIs, and security models.
Experience with SQL, relational databases, and data integration.
Experience creating dashboards/Business Intelligence based on key performance indicators.
Experience providing custom solution client-side development and maintenance using Power Automate / Power Apps.
Experience providing user training, provisioning, and management of SharePoint solutions.
A team player with solid communication skills, excellent at problem solving, troubleshooting, and embracing new challenges.
Positive attitude and the ability to remain flexible in a dynamic environment.
This position requires a Secret clearance.

Benefits
Competitive salary based on experience, up to $160,000.
Flexible work hours and the ability to work remotely.
Opportunities for professional development and certification.
Collaborative environment with a focus on innovative solutions.
Engagement in impactful projects that benefit society.
Health and wellness benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.

title: Remote Associate Contract Attorney
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Contract Attorney - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in enhancing the legal framework for technology and infrastructure contracts. You will collaborate with various stakeholders, ensuring that contractual obligations are clearly defined and executed. This position requires a blend of legal expertise and collaborative skills to navigate the complexities of technology transactions. You'll provide essential support to ensure compliance with regulations and optimize legal processes. Your contributions will directly impact our partner’s operational efficiency and contractual success while working flexibly in a remote setting.

Provide transactional legal support across technology infrastructure initiatives.
Partner with in-house counsel and business stakeholders to deliver timely, business-focused solutions.
Review and redline contracts to align with corporate legal and business requirements.
Identify and escalate legal risks, ensuring compliance with client standards and playbooks.
Collaborate with leadership on workflow improvements and knowledge management.
Support quality control (QC) efforts, audits, and process documentation as needed.

Prior in-house, managed services, or law firm experience with technology clients preferred.
Strong drafting, redlining, and negotiation skills.
Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently while collaborating with remote teams.
Comfortable working across time zones; availability to cover Pacific Time zone required.

Health, vision, and dental benefits.

title: Remote Assistant Controller Sr.
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture

Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

title: Remote Assistant Controller - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture
Health and wellness programs
Work-life balance support
Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

\n

description_text:

This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation through the Microsoft Power Platform. You will lead the development and delivery of impactful solutions that enhance the agency's efficiency. title: North America Legislation Content Specialist - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a North America Legislation Content Specialist - REMOTE. This role plays a crucial part as a regional subject matter expert for legislation in the US and Canada. The specialist will lead various legislative projects, ensuring the content aligns with global strategies and user needs. With a strong focus on editorial workflows, the role demands collaboration with cross-functional teams to optimize tools and ensure high-quality legislative datasets. Contributing to the integration of automation and advanced technologies, this position is essential for the modernization of legislative workflows.
\n

Accountabilities
Act as the primary SME for legislation across North America and Canada, maintaining deep knowledge of local frameworks.
Advise across multiple projects on authoritative sources, acquisition models, and jurisdictional nuances.
Define content acquisition requirements and ensure reliable source coverage aligned with global standards.
Provide expert guidance on conversion rules and publication workflows.
Develop and implement a content strategy for each jurisdiction aligned with customer needs.
Identify new legislative opportunities and improvements in coverage.
Translate legislative and editorial requirements into actionable backlogs.
Lead defining editorial requirements for CMSs and workflows.
Produce high-quality editorial effort estimates.
Identify scope risks and communicate them clearly.
Work closely with GTO, Product, and Technology to optimize workflows.
Test new tools and provide actionable feedback.
Coordinate editorial expertise across multiple projects.
Participate in agile ceremonies and manage dependencies.

Requirements
Law degree (or equivalent).
Minimum 5 years’ experience in legal research or legislative content management.
Proven ability to lead agile ceremonies.
Demonstrated AI fluency with practical experience in AI tools.
Strong understanding of legislative frameworks.
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills.
Strong leadership and collaboration skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement abilities.
Highly organized and detail-oriented.
Project management experience (e.g., Agile, Scrum) desirable.

Benefits
Full-time, hybrid or homeworking position.
Global collaboration across time zones.
Country-specific benefits available.

title: Contract Attorney - Remote
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Contract Attorney - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in enhancing the legal framework for technology and infrastructure contracts. You will collaborate with various stakeholders, ensuring that contractual obligations are clearly defined and executed. This position requires a blend of legal expertise and collaborative skills to navigate the complexities of technology transactions. You'll provide essential support to ensure compliance with regulations and optimize legal processes. Your contributions will directly impact our partner’s operational efficiency and contractual success while working flexibly in a remote setting.
\n

Accountabilities
Draft, review, and negotiate a variety of technology and infrastructure contracts, including vendor agreements, hardware supply, and engineering support deals.
Provide transactional legal support across technology infrastructure initiatives.
Partner with in-house counsel and business stakeholders to deliver timely, business-focused solutions.
Review and redline contracts to align with corporate legal and business requirements.
Identify and escalate legal risks, ensuring compliance with client standards and playbooks.
Collaborate with leadership on workflow improvements and knowledge management.
Support quality control (QC) efforts, audits, and process documentation as needed.

Requirements
Prior in-house, managed services, or law firm experience with technology clients preferred.
Strong drafting, redlining, and negotiation skills.
Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently while collaborating with remote teams.
Comfortable working across time zones; availability to cover Pacific Time zone required.

Benefits
Health, vision, and dental benefits.

Tuition discounts for continued education.
Flexible work schedule in a remote setting.
Collaborative team environment.

title: Lead Executive Area Manager - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Executive Area Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will be responsible for driving sales and implementing effective strategies to enhance market penetration. You will work closely with various teams to ensure alignment with the company’s mission and objectives while providing exceptional support to clients. Your work will directly contribute to the growth and success of the organization, ultimately making a significant impact on healthcare innovations. The position allows for flexibility to operate from a remote setting, creating a balanced work-life experience.
\n

Identify targets and execute selling strategies based on company direction through ethical selling practice.
Drive sales growth by implementing best practices to ensure proper targeting and reimbursement strategies.
Contribute key learnings to demonstrate a deep understanding of marketplace opportunities.
Utilize approved corporate literature and scientific data in communications with clients.
Provide reporting and tracking of sales activities along with territory forecasts.
Maintain a solid understanding of industry products and market trends.
Ensure compliance with company guidelines and relevant laws.
Assist with training and mentoring new team members.

Requirements
BS/BA in a related discipline or equivalent experience required.
5+ years of experience in the wound care field and medical sales is required.
Experience in medical devices and tissue biologics preferred.
Surgical experience and O.R./VAC Submission experience required.
Familiarity with skin substitutes is a plus.
Previous experience in startup and medium-sized companies advantageous.

title: Associate Attorney - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Contract Attorney - REMOTE. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in enhancing the legal framework for technology and infrastructure contracts. You will collaborate with various stakeholders, ensuring that contractual obligations are clearly defined and executed. This position requires a blend of legal expertise and collaborative skills to navigate the complexities of technology transactions. You'll provide essential support to ensure compliance with regulations and optimize legal processes. Your contributions will directly impact our partner’s operational efficiency and contractual success while working flexibly in a remote setting.
\n

Accountabilities
Draft, review, and negotiate a variety of technology and infrastructure contracts, including vendor agreements, hardware supply, and engineering support deals.
Provide transactional legal support across technology infrastructure initiatives.
Partner with in-house counsel and business stakeholders to deliver timely, business-focused solutions.
Review and redline contracts to align with corporate legal and business requirements.
Identify and escalate legal risks, ensuring compliance with client standards and playbooks.
Collaborate with leadership on workflow improvements and knowledge management.
Support quality control (QC) efforts, audits, and process documentation as needed.

Requirements
Prior in-house, managed services, or law firm experience with technology clients preferred.
Strong drafting, redlining, and negotiation skills.
Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently while collaborating with remote teams.
Comfortable working across time zones; availability to cover Pacific Time zone required.

Benefits
Health, vision, and dental benefits.
401k retirement plan.
Tuition discounts for continued education.
Flexible work schedule in a remote setting.
Collaborative team environment.

title: Finance Manager - Remote
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

\n

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Power Apps Developer (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Power Apps Developer - REMOTE. This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation through the Microsoft Power Platform. You will lead the development and delivery of impactful solutions that enhance the agency's efficiency. The ideal candidate will work closely with stakeholders to ensure that their requirements are met effectively. With an emphasis on collaboration and innovation, this position not only focuses on technical development but also on improving user experiences across various applications. Join us to contribute to significant projects that make a difference in the public sector.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the design, development, and delivery of solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, analyze, and translate requirements into functional solutions and data visualization.
Provide solution architecture guidance and ensure scalability, security, and maintainability of applications.
Plan, create, configure, customize, and resolve issues related to SharePoint sites based on user requirements and security models.
Modify and update Power Apps forms as new information is released.
Document site updates and inform users on enhancements.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to Power Apps applications and integrations.
Implement and Configure Microsoft Dataverse and Connectors to support Power Platform solutions.
Stay current on Power Platform updates and emerging technologies to recommend innovative improvements.

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.
Eight (8) years’ experience in Microsoft technologies, including 3+ years of hands-on Power Platform development (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI).
Strong understanding of solution architecture, data integration, connectors, APIs, and security models.
Experience with SQL, relational databases, and data integration.
Experience creating dashboards/Business Intelligence based on key performance indicators.
Experience providing custom solution client-side development and maintenance using Power Automate / Power Apps.
Experience providing user training, provisioning, and management of SharePoint solutions.
A team player with solid communication skills, excellent at problem solving, troubleshooting, and embracing new challenges.
Positive attitude and the ability to remain flexible in a dynamic environment.
This position requires a Secret clearance.

Benefits
Competitive salary based on experience, up to $160,000.
Flexible work hours and the ability to work remotely.
Opportunities for professional development and certification.
Collaborative environment with a focus on innovative solutions.
Engagement in impactful projects that benefit society.
Health and wellness benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.

title: Finance Specialist - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Lead Assistant Controller (REMOTE)
locations_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture
Health and wellness programs
Work-life balance support
Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation through the Microsoft Power Platform. You will lead the development and delivery of impactful solutions that enhance the agency's efficiency. title: Lead Area Manager - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Executive Area Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will be responsible for driving sales and implementing effective strategies to enhance market penetration. You will work closely with various teams to ensure alignment with the company’s mission and objectives while providing exceptional support to clients. Your work will directly contribute to the growth and success of the organization, ultimately making a significant impact on healthcare innovations. The position allows for flexibility to operate from a remote setting, creating a balanced work-life experience.
\n

Accountabilities
Identify targets and execute selling strategies based on company direction through ethical selling practice.
Drive sales growth by implementing best practices to ensure proper targeting and reimbursement strategies.
Contribute key learnings to demonstrate a deep understanding of marketplace opportunities.
Utilize approved corporate literature and scientific data in communications with clients.
Provide reporting and tracking of sales activities along with territory forecasts.
Maintain a solid understanding of industry products and market trends.
Ensure compliance with company guidelines and relevant laws.
Assist with training and mentoring new team members.

Requirements
BS/BA in a related discipline or equivalent experience required.
5+ years of experience in the wound care field and medical sales is required.
Experience in medical devices and tissue biologics preferred.
Surgical experience and O.R./VAC Submission experience required.
Familiarity with skin substitutes is a plus.
Previous experience in startup and medium-sized companies advantageous.

Opportunities for professional development and training.

Health and wellness benefits.

title: Lead Assistant Controller - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture
Health and wellness programs
Work-life balance support
Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

title: Lead Change Management Specialist (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Change Management Consultant - REMOTE. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in guiding organizations through their digital transformation journey using Microsoft 365 technologies. Your expertise in Organizational Change Management (OCM) will directly impact the success of our clients and their ability to adopt new technologies effectively. By collaborating with cross-functional teams, you will develop and implement OCM strategies that address the unique needs of each client. Your work will not only enhance user adoption but also empower teams to utilize tools like Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive to their full potential. Join us to make a significant impact and drive meaningful change within organizations.
\n

Accountabilities
Work with the OCM Practice Manager and elite team on various projects.
Conduct OCM activities including vision discovery and change scope analysis.
Develop and implement new offerings related to Microsoft Copilot.
Strategize communication and training plans for digital transformation.
Educate stakeholders and users to ease the transition.
Measure and report the success of change initiatives.
Design change management plans using best practices.
Provide leadership and guidance to junior consultants.
Collaborate with project teams to align OCM efforts with project objectives.
Stay updated on emerging technologies to create new OCM materials.

Requirements
5+ years of experience in change management or adoption functions.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365 technologies including Copilot, Teams, and SharePoint.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.
Experience with user research and post-implementation reviews.
Ability to proactively respond to internal/external inquiries.
Demonstrated expertise in OCM projects applied to Microsoft 365.
Experience with GCC High migration projects is a plus.
Preferred credentials include PROSCI Certification or Microsoft Adoption Specialist certification.
Proven ability to lead assessments with various levels of stakeholders.
Track record of delivering results and adapting to diverse cultures.

Benefits
Be part of a forward-thinking company that values innovation.
Work with a passionate team dedicated to making a difference.
Enjoy a flexible remote work environment.
Access to ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
Collaborate in a supportive and inclusive remote team.

title: Financial Management Lead - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Finance Operations Specialist - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. \n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.

Remote work options within the United States and its territories.

title: Executive Area Manager - Remote Work
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Executive Area Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will be responsible for driving sales and implementing effective strategies to enhance market penetration. You will work closely with various teams to ensure alignment with the company’s mission and objectives while providing exceptional support to clients. Your work will directly contribute to the growth and success of the organization, ultimately making a significant impact on healthcare innovations. The position allows for flexibility to operate from a remote setting, creating a balanced work-life experience.
\n

Accountabilities
Identify targets and execute selling strategies based on company direction through ethical selling practice.
Drive sales growth by implementing best practices to ensure proper targeting and reimbursement strategies.
Contribute key learnings to demonstrate a deep understanding of marketplace opportunities.
Utilize approved corporate literature and scientific data in communications with clients.
Provide reporting and tracking of sales activities along with territory forecasts.
Maintain a solid understanding of industry products and market trends.
Ensure compliance with company guidelines and relevant laws.
Assist with training and mentoring new team members.

Requirements
BS/BA in a related discipline or equivalent experience required.
5+ years of experience in the wound care field and medical sales is required.
Experience in medical devices and tissue biologics preferred.
Surgical experience and O.R./VAC Submission experience required.
Familiarity with skin substitutes is a plus.
Previous experience in startup and medium-sized companies advantageous.

Flexible work hours and remote work opportunities.
Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
Opportunities for professional development and training.
A diverse and inclusive work environment.
Health and wellness benefits.

title: Assistant Controller (Remote)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Benefits
Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Flexible working environment with remote work options
Opportunities for professional development and growth
Collaborative and innovative team culture
Health and wellness programs
Work-life balance support
Access to cutting-edge technology and tools

\n

You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Advertising Finance Consultant - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Finance Manager - REMOTE. In this role, you will have the opportunity to make a significant impact by ensuring financial accuracy and supporting the mission of our nonprofit clients. You will collaborate closely with various teams, providing essential financial insights and analysis to drive informed decision-making. Your work will contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of operations, allowing our partner to focus on critical social justice issues. Embracing a commitment to anti-racism and inclusion, you'll be surrounded by a supportive team dedicated to achieving meaningful outcomes while ensuring a collaborative and inclusive environment. Join us in making a difference while enjoying the flexibility of remote work.
\n

Accountabilities
Lead the annual media budgeting process and quarterly financial forecast tracking.
Manage the monthly financial close process for media spend to ensure accuracy.
Provide variance analysis of actual spend versus budget/forecast.
Oversee and manage monthly media expenditure and vendor invoice processing.
Ensure timely reconciliation of media plans against actual spend.
Provide real-time media spend balances to the Advertising team.
Prepare and present executive-level reporting on media spend and performance.
Act as the primary financial liaison for Advertising leadership and finance.
Identify and implement improvements to media financial processes and reporting tools.

Requirements
7-9 years of experience in Accounting or Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A).
Experience working with marketing or media teams.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Proficiency in Google Suite products.
Experience with financial planning software and AP invoice processing systems.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple projects under tight deadlines.

Benefits
36-hour workweek with half-day Fridays and scheduling flexibility.
Three weeks of vacation and 17 paid sick days.
Fully paid medical, dental, and vision benefits for individual employees.
401(k) plan with discretionary profit-sharing contributions.
Remote work options within the United States and its territories.
Annual $1,250 professional development stipend.
Flexible Spending Accounts for medical and dependent care.
11 holidays and a Mid-summer break during the week of July 4.
Exceptional paid leave policies for parental, medical, and caregiving responsibilities.

title: Assistant Controller - REMOTE
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Remote Assistant Controller. In this role, you will have a critical impact on the company's financial success, managing accounting operations and supporting financial reporting. You will collaborate closely with leadership, utilizing your expertise to lead and mentor the team through complex financial challenges. This is an opportunity to shape the future of robotic deliveries while ensuring compliance with accounting standards and regulations. Your work will directly contribute to enhancing operational efficiency within a dynamic and fast-paced organization.
\n

Accountabilities
Manage and execute the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes
Mentor and develop accounting team members through regular coaching and performance feedback
Oversee the consolidation of global subsidiaries, including foreign currency translation
Ensure timely payments for Accounts Payable and timely receipt of invoices for Accounts Receivable
Manage accounting for property, plant, and equipment, including capitalization policies and impairments
Handle accounting for leases under ASC 842, including ROU assets and lease liabilities
Coordinate with external and internal auditors and manage audit deliverables
Maintain process documentation and narratives
Help scale accounting systems, tools, and workflows to support company growth
Support in year-end inventory count (travel may be required)
Foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement
Act as a trusted backup to the Controller
Project management of ad hoc projects

Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance
7+ years of progressive accounting experience
4-5 years of experience managing and leading a team
Combination of Big 4 public accounting and industry experience
Experience working at or supporting a public company
Strong knowledge of US GAAP and SEC reporting requirements
Hands-on experience with SOX compliance
Advanced financial technology skills; experience with modern ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite)

Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
